rowAttribs.description: The set of row attributes . Accessed by row index. The members of these attributes can be directly written to / updated with new values and take priority over any attribute members defined a the table level. n . rowAttribs [ 3 ] . rowHeight = 50

cellAttribs.description: The set of cell attributes . Accessed by row and column. The members of these attributes can be directly written to / updated with new values and take priority over any attribute members defined at the row/col or table level. n . cellAttribs [ 3 , 4 ] . text = 'Fade' n . cellAttribs [ 3 , 4 ] . bgColor = ( 0.5 , 0 , 0 , 1 ) #highlight this cell red

displayAttribs.description: The set of attributes actually displayed in the cell. They are a combination of the cell/row/col/table attributes described above. Accessed by row and column. When combining attributes, cell attributes take priority over row and column attributes, which themselves take priority over table attributes. n . displayAttribs [ 3 , 4 ] . bgColor #the resulting background color for this specific cell

focusCol.description: Last column with focus for editing.

focusRow.description: Last row with focus for editing.

selectionBorderColor.description: Get or set the border color for the separate selection, expressed as a 4-tuple (r,g,b,a), representing its red, green, blue and alpha value.

selectionColor.description: Get or set the background color for the separate selection, expressed as a 4-tuple (r,g,b,a), representing its red, green, blue and alpha value.

selections.description: Get or set the row and column coordinates for separate selection formatting, expressed as a list of 4-tuples, each containing: (startrow, startcol, endrow, endcol).

dropRow.description: The current row about to be dropped over.

dropCol.description: The current col about to be dropped over.

scroll.description: Scroll List component to the row and column specified. row, col - The row and column to scroll to.

setKeyboardFocus.description: Selects and sets the keyboard focus in a cell of the table if the cell is a field. row, col - The row and column of the cell to set the keyboard focus. selectAll - (Keyword, Optional) If True, then all text will be selected.

reset.description: Reset the list by running its initialize callbacks.

displlayColWidth.description: Return the actual column width including stretching.

displayRowHeight.description: Return the actual row height including stretching.

MAT.description: A MAT describes a reference to a MAT operator.

MOD.description: The MOD class provides access to Module On Demand object, which allows DATs to be dynamically imported as modules. It can be accessed with the mod object, found in the automatically imported td module . Alternatively, one can use the regular python statement: import. Use of the import statement is l...

Matrix.description: The matrix class holds a single 4x4 matrix for use in transformations. The matrix's data layout is in column-major format , which is to say that the matrix is multiplied from the left of vectors and positions . The translation values are stored in the last column of the matrix.

vals.description: Get or set the set of Matrix values.

rows.description: The list of Matrix rows, each a list of values.

cols.description: The list of Matrix columns, each a list of values.

transpose.description: Transpose the values in the matrix. m . transpose () # m now contains the transpose of the matrix

getTranspose.description: Returns the transpose of the matrix, leaving the matrix itself unchanged. m2 = m . getTranspose ()

invert.description: Inverts the values in the matrix. m . invert () # m now contains the inverse of the matrix

getInverse.description: Returns the inverse of the matrix, leaving the matrix itself unchanged. m2 = m . getInverse ()

determinant.description: Returns the determinant of the matrix. l = m . determinant ()

mapUnitSquareToQuad.signature: mapUnitSquareToQuad(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
mapUnitSquareToQuad.description: Set the matrix to be a projection matrix that maps coordinates from to a unit square (0,0) -> (1,1) space to a space defined by an arbitrary quadrilateral (blX, blY) -> (trX, trY). The 4 corners of the quadrilateral are given ('bl' means bottom left, 'tr' means top right etc.).

mapQuadToUnitSquare.signature: mapQuadToUnitSquare(blX, blY, brX, brY, tlX, tlY, trX, trY)
mapQuadToUnitSquare.description: Is the inverse of mapUnitSquareToQuad(). Mapping coordinates in an arbitrary quadrilateral into a space defined by the unit square.

fillTable.description: Fill in the contents of a table from the matrix which the method is called upon. tableDAT - The table to be filled.

numpyArray.description: Returns this matrix as a 4x4 NumPy array.

identity.description: Replaces the values in the matrix with the identity matrix . m . identity () # now contains the identity matrix

copy.description: Returns a new matrix that is a copy of the matrix. newM = m . copy () # newM will have the same values as m, m is unchanged

translate.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new translation matrix created from tx, ty and tz. The translation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if T is the new translation matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = T * M. tx, ty, tz - The translation value in each axis. fromRight - (Keyword, Optional) If True, the translation matrix will be multiplied from the right instead of the left. m = tdu . Matrix () m . translate ( 5 , 0 , 10 )

rotate.description: Multiplies the current matrix by 3 rotation matrices, first a rotation around the X axis by rx degrees, followed by a rotation around the Y axis by ry degrees, followed by the same for rz. The rotation values are in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. So if M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = RZ * RY * RX * M. rx, ry, rz - The rotation value around each X, Y and Z axis. The value is in degrees. The rotation is applied in XYZ orde

rotateOnAxis.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new rotation matrix created by rotation angle degrees around the axis specified by rotationAxis. The angle is in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if R is the new rotation matrix specified by rotationAxis and angle, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = R * M. rotationAxis - A axis to rotate around. This should be a Vector or a list with 3 entries. It does not need to be norm

scale.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a scale matrix created from sx, sy and sz. The scale is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if S is the new scale matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = S * M. sx, sy, sz - The scale value along each X, Y and Z axis. fromRight - (Keyword, Optional) If True, the scale matrix will be multiplied from the right instead of the left. pivot - (Keyword, Optional) If given, the scale will be applied around

lookat.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a lookat matrix created using the given values to the matrix. The lookat matrix is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if L is the new lookat matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = L * M. The values for to parameters can be given as anything that can be treated as a list of 3 values. E.g a TDU.Vector, TDU.Position or simply a list of size 3. eyePos - The position in space of the eye/camera. target

decompose.description: Decomposes the matrix into its scale, rotate and translate values. These are the same as the translate, rotate and scale that are in the Geometry COMP and other Object components. However due to rotations being able to be solved in different ways, it's likely a decomposed transform matrix from a Geometry COMP will not have the same values as its parameter. The resulting transform is the same though. This function returns a tuple of tuples (3 tuples), which are the scale, rotate and translate val

projectionFrustum.signature: projectionFrustum(left, right, bottom, top, near, far)
projectionFrustum.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix using the given frustum extents. The left, right, bottom, top extents are located on the near plane. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

projectionFovX.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix defined by the FOV(given in degrees), an aspect ratio and near/far planes. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan. fovX - The horizontal FOV, specified in degrees. aspectX, aspectY - The aspect ration values. These can be something like 16 and 9 for an aspect or the render resolution such as 1920 and 1080. The results will be the same for the same ratio.

projectAndClip.description: Applies a projection matrix to a position if it's within the normalized clip space. Returns None if projected position is clipped. pos - The position to be projected.

projectionStereo.signature: projectionStereo(ipd : float, convergeZ : float, fovX : float, aspectX : float, aspectY : float, near : float, far : float, rightEye : bool=False)
projectionStereo.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with an asymetrical projection matrix suitable for stereo rendering. The left eye's projection matrix is given by default, set rightEye=True to get the right eye's instead. For proper rendering, the cameras will also need to be translated in X by -ipd/2 and +ipd/2 for the left and right eyes respectively. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan. ipd - Interpupillary distance of the user, generally speci

[].description: Gets or sets the specified entry in the matrix. tx = m [ 0 , 3 ] m [ 0 , 3 ] = tx + 5

Matrix.description: Multiplies the position by the matrix and returns the a new position as the result. If the matrix was not an transformation matrix, such as a projection matrix instead, the perspective divide by W will automatically be applied to X, Y and Z. newP = M * p

Mesh.description: A Mesh describes an instance of a single geometry mesh . It is an instance of a Prim Class .

closedU.description: Returns True if the mesh is closed in U, False otherwise.

closedV.description: Returns True if the mesh is closed in V, False otherwise.

numRows.description: Number of rows in the mesh.

numCols.description: Number of columns in the mesh.

Monitor.description: The Monitor class describes a single instance of a monitor display. They can be accessed from the monitors object.

index.description: The monitor position in the list.

isPrimary.description: Returns true, if this monitor is the primary display.

isAffinity.description: Returns true, if this monitor is connected to the GPU that has been selected for GPU Affinity. Always True if GPU Affinity is not used.

connectedGPUName.description: The name associated with this monitor's GPU .

connectedGPUIndex.description: The index of this monitor's GPU .

width.description: The width of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

height.description: The height of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

left.description: The position of left edge of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

right.description: The position of right edge of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

top.description: The position of top edge of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

bottom.description: The position of bottom edge of the monitor area, measured in pixels.

displayName.description: The unique display name associated with this monitor.

description.description: A description of the monitor or its display adapter.

dpiScale.description: The DPI Scaling factor the monitor is current set to.

scaledWidth.description: The width of the monitor area, measured in points.

scaledHeight.description: The height of the monitor area, measured in points.

scaledLeft.description: The position of left edge of the monitor area, measured in points.

scaledRight.description: The position of right edge of the monitor area, measured in points.

scaledTop.description: The position of top edge of the monitor area, measured in points.

scaledBottom.description: The position of bottom edge of the monitor area, measured in points.

serialNumber.description: The serial number name associated with this monitor. May be blank.

refreshRate.description: The refresh rate the monitor is currently running at.

Monitors.description: The Monitors class describes the set of all installed monitor objects . It can be accessed with the monitors object, found in the automatically imported td module . It operates much like a Python list of monitor objects.

width.description: The width of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

height.description: The height of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

left.description: The leftmost edge of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

right.description: The rightmost edge of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

top.description: The topmost position of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

bottom.description: The bottommost position of the combined monitor area, measured in pixels.

locate.description: Return the monitor at the specified mouse coordinates, or None.

refresh.description: Causes the application to behave as if a monitor device has changed. Monitors DAT and other sources will be updated. This is typically done automatically by the operating system, but in special cases can be triggered manually with this method.

NetworkEditor.description: The NetworkEditor class describes an instance of a Network Editor . They are subclasses of the Pane Class , which can be accessed from the ui object.

showBackdropCHOPs.description: Enable or disable CHOP viewers as backdrops.

showBackdropGeometry.description: Enable or disable SOP and Geometry object viewers as backdrops.

showBackdropTOPs.description: Enable or disable TOP viewers as backdrops.

showColorPalette.description: Enable or disable display of the operator color palette selector.

showDataLinks.description: Enable or disable operator data links.

showList.description: Control display of operators as a list, or connected nodes.

showNetworkOverview.description: Enable or disable display of the network overview.

showParameters.description: Enable or disable display of the currently selected operator parameters.

straightLinks.description: Control display of operator links as straight or curved.

x.description: Get or set the x coordinate of the network editor area, where 1 unit = 1 pixel when zoom = 1.

y.description: Get or set the y coordinate of the network editor area, where 1 unit = 1 pixel when zoom = 1.

zoom.description: Get or set the zoom factor of the network editor area, where a zoom factor of 1 draws each node at its unscaled resolution.

fitWidth.description: Fit the network area to specified width, specified in node units. This affects the zoom factor. width - The width to fit to.

fitHeight.description: Fit the network area to specified height, specified in node units. This affects the zoom factor. height - The height to fit to.

home.description: Home all operators in the network. zoom - (Keyword, Optional) When true, the view will be scaled accordingly, otherwise the nodes will only be re-centered. op - (Keyword, Optional) If an operator is specified, the network will be homed around its location. p = ui . panes [ 'pane1' ] n = op ( '/project1' ) p . home ( op = n ) p = ui . panes [ 2 ] p . home ( zoom = True )

homeSelected.description: Home all selected operators in the network. zoom - (Keyword, Optional) When true, the view will be scaled accordingly, otherwise the nodes will only be re-centered.

placeOPs.description: Use the mouse to place the specified operators in the pane. listOfOps - The list of operators to be placed. inputIndex - If specified, which input index to connect to. outputIndex - If specified, which output index to connect to. delOP - If specified, deletes that operator immediately after placing the listOfOPs. undoName - Describes the Undo operation.

OP.description: The OP class defines a reference to a single operator .

name.description: Get or set the operator name.

base.description: Returns the beginning portion of the name occurring before any digits.

digits.description: Returns the numeric value of the last consecutive group of digits in the name, or None if not found. The digits can be in the middle of the name if there are none at the end of the name.

id.description: Unique id for the operator. This id can also be passed to the op() and ops() shortcuts. Id's are not consistent when a file is re-opened, and will change if the OP is copied/pasted, changes OP types, deleted/undone. The id will not change if the OP is renamed though. Its data type is integer.

op.description: The operator finder object, for accessing operators through paths or shortcuts. Note: a version of this method that searches relative to '/' is also in the global td module . op(pattern1, pattern2..., includeUtility=False) → OP or None Returns the first OP whose path matches the given pattern, relative to the inside of this operator. Will return None if nothing is found. Multiple patterns may be specified which are all added to the search. Numeric OP ids may also be used. pattern - Can be string

opex.description: An operator finder object, for accessing operators through paths or shortcuts. Works like the op() shortcut method, except it will raise an exception if it fails to find the node instead of returning None as op() does. This is now the recommended way to get nodes in parameter expressions, as the error will be more useful than, for example, NoneType has no attribute "par" , that is often seen when using op(). Note: a version of this method that searches relative to '/' is also in the global td mo

parent.description: The Parent Shortcut object, for accessing parent components through indices or shortcuts. Note: a version of this method that searches relative to the current operator is also in the global td module . parent(n) → OP or None The nth parent of this operator. If n not specified, returns the parent. If n = 2, returns the parent of the parent, etc. If no parent exists at that level, None is returned. n - (Optional) n is the number of levels up to climb. When n = 1 it will return the operator's paren

iop.description: The Internal Operator Shortcut object, for accessing internal shortcuts. See also Internal Operators . Note: a version of this method that searches relative to the current operator is also in the global td Module .

ipar.description: The Internal Operator Parameter Shortcut object, for accessing internal shortcuts. See also Internal Parameters . Note: a version of this method that searches relative to the current operator is also in the global td Module .

valid.description: True if the referenced operator currently exists, False if it has been deleted.

supported.description: True if supported on the current Operating System.

passive.description: If true, operator will not cook before its access methods are called. To use a passive version of an operator n, use passive(n).

time.description: Time Component that defines the operator's time reference.

ext.description: Object that searches for parent extensions . me . ext . MyClass

mod.description: Get a module on demand object that searches for DAT modules relative to this operator.

fileFolder.description: Returns the folder where this node is saved.

filePath.description: Returns the file location of this node.

replicator.description: The replicatorCOMP that created this operator, if any.

storage.description: Storage is dictionary associated with this operator. Values stored in this dictionary are persistent, and saved with the operator. The dictionary attribute is read only, but not its contents. Its contents may be manipulated directly with methods such as OP .fetch() or OP .store() described below, or examined with an Examine DAT .

tags.description: Get or set a set of user defined strings. Tags can be searched using OP .findChildren() and the OP Find DAT . The set is a regular python set, and can be accessed accordingly: n . tags = [ 'effect' , 'image filter' ] n . tags . add ( 'darken' )

children.description: A list of operators contained within this operator. Only component operators have children, otherwise an empty list is returned.

numChildren.description: Returns the number of children contained within the operator. Only component operators have children.

numChildrenRecursive.description: Returns the number of operators contained recursively within this operator. Only component operators have children.

enclosedBy.description: The (possibly empty) list of Annotate operators enclosing this node. See also AnnotateCOMP.enclosedOPs .

par.description: An intermediate parameter collection object, from which a specific parameter can be found. n . par . tx # or n . par [ 'tx' ]

parGroup.description: An intermediate parameter collection object, from which a specific parameter group can be found. n . parGroup . t # or n . parGroup [ 't' ]

curPar.description: The parameter currently being evaluated. Can be used in a parameter expression to reference itself. An easy way to see this is to put the expression curPar.name in any string parameter.

builtinPars.description: A list of all built-in parameters .

customPars.description: A list of all custom parameters .

customParGroups.description: A list of all ParGroups , where a ParGroup is a set of parameters all drawn on the same line of a dialog, sharing the same label.

seq.description: An intermediate sequence collection object, from which a specific sequence can be found. comp . seq . ext # or comp . seq [ 'ext' ]

curBlock.description: The SequenceBlock of the parameter currently being evaluated. Can be used in a parameter expression to reference itself.

curSeq.description: The Sequence of the parameter currently being evaluated. Can be used in a parameter expression to reference itself.

pages.description: A list of all built-in pages.

customPages.description: A list of all custom pages .

currentPage.description: Get or set the currently displayed parameter page. It can be set by setting it to another page or a string label. n . currentPage = 'Common'

activeViewer.description: Get or set Viewer Active Flag .

allowCooking.description: Get or set Cooking Flag . Only COMPs can disable this flag.

cloneImmune.description: Get or set Clone Immune Flag .

expose.description: Get or set the Expose Flag which hides a node from view in a network.

selected.description: Get or set Selected Flag . This controls if the node is part of the network selection. (yellow box around it).

python.description: Get or set parameter expression language as python.

showCustomOnly.description: Get or set the Show Custom Only Flag which controls whether or not non custom parameters are display in parameter dialogs .

showDocked.description: Get or set Show Docked Flag . This controls whether this node is visible or hidden when it is docked to another node.

color.description: Get or set color value, expressed as a 3-tuple, representing its red, green, blue values. To convert between color spaces, use the built in colorsys module.

nodeHeight.description: Get or set node height, expressed in network editor units.

nodeWidth.description: Get or set node width, expressed in network editor units.

nodeX.description: Get or set node X value, expressed in network editor units, measured from its left edge.

nodeY.description: Get or set node Y value, expressed in network editor units, measured from its bottom edge.

nodeCenterX.description: Get or set node X value, expressed in network editor units, measured from its center.

nodeCenterY.description: Get or set node Y value, expressed in network editor units, measured from its center.

dock.description: Get or set the operator this operator is docked to. To clear docking, set this member to None.

docked.description: The (possibly empty) list of operators docked to this node.

inputs.description: List of input operators (via left side connectors) to this operator. To get the number of inputs, use len( OP .inputs).

outputs.description: List of output operators (via right side connectors) from this operator.

inputConnectors.description: List of input connectors (on the left side) associated with this operator.

outputConnectors.description: List of output connectors (on the right side) associated with this operator.

cookFrame.description: Last frame at which this operator cooked.

cookTime.description: Deprecated Duration of the last measured cook (in milliseconds).

cpuCookTime.description: Duration of the last measured cook in CPU time (in milliseconds).

cookAbsFrame.description: Last absolute frame at which this operator cooked.

cookStartTime.description: Last offset from frame start at which this operator cook began, expressed in milliseconds.

cookEndTime.description: Last offset from frame start at which this operator cook ended, expressed in milliseconds. Other operators may have cooked between the start and end time. See the cookTime member for this operator's specific cook duration.

cookedThisFrame.description: True when this operator has cooked this frame.

cookedPreviousFrame.description: True when this operator has cooked the previous frame.

childrenCookTime.description: Deprecated The total accumulated cook time of all children of this operator during the last frame. Zero if the operator is not a COMP and/or has no children.

childrenCPUCookTime.description: The total accumulated cook time of all children of this operator during the last frame. Zero if the operator is not a COMP and/or has no children.

childrenCookAbsFrame.description: Deprecated The absolute frame on which childrenCookTime is based.

childrenCPUCookAbsFrame.description: The absolute frame on which childrenCPUCookTime is based.

gpuCookTime.description: Duration of GPU operations during the last measured cook (in milliseconds).

childrenGPUCookTime.description: The total accumulated GPU cook time of all children of this operator during the last frame. Zero if the operator is not a COMP and/or has no children.

childrenGPUCookAbsFrame.description: The absolute frame on which childrenGPUCookTime is based.

totalCooks.description: Number of times the operator has cooked.

cpuMemory.description: The approximate amount of CPU memory this Operator is using, in bytes.

gpuMemory.description: The amount of GPU memory this OP is using, in bytes.

type.description: Operator type as a string. Example: 'oscin'.

subType.description: Operator subtype. Currently only implemented for components . May be one of: 'panel', 'object', or empty string in the case of base components.

opType.description: Python operator class type, as a string. Example: 'oscinCHOP'. Can be used with COMP .create() method.

icon.description: Get the letters used to create the operator's icon.

family.description: Operator family. Example: CHOP . Use the global dictionary families for a list of each operator type.

isFilter.description: True if operator is a filter, false if it is a generator.

minInputs.description: Minimum number of inputs to the operator.

maxInputs.description: Maximum number of inputs to the operator.

isMultiInputs.description: True if inputs are ordered, false otherwise. Operators with an arbitrary number of inputs have unordered inputs, example Merge CHOP .

visibleLevel.description: Visibility level of the operator. For example, expert operators have visibility level 1, regular operators have visibility level 0.

isBase.description: True if the operator is a Base (miscellaneous) component .

isCHOP.description: True if the operator is a CHOP .

isCOMP.description: True if the operator is a component .

isDAT.description: True if the operator is a DAT .

isMAT.description: True if the operator is a Material .

isObject.description: True if the operator is an object .

isPanel.description: True if the operator is a Panel .

isSOP.description: True if the operator is a SOP .

isTOP.description: True if the operators is a TOP .

isPOP.description: True if the operators is a POP .

isCustom.description: True if the operators is a Custom Operator .

licenseType.description: Type of License required for the operator.

pars.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of parameter objects that match the pattern. pattern - Is a string following the Pattern Matching rules, specifying which parameters to return. newlist = op ( 'geo1' ) . pars ( 't?' , 'r?' , 's?' ) #translate/rotate/scale parameters Note: If searching for a single parameter given a name, it's much more efficient to use the subscript operator. For example: name = 'MyName1' op ( 'geo1' ) . par [ name ]

parGroups.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of parGroup objects that match the pattern. pattern - Is a string following the Pattern Matching rules, specifying which parameters to return. debug ( op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroups ( 'p*' )) Note: If searching for a single ParGroup given a name, it's much more efficient to use the subscript operator. For example: name = 'MyColor' op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup [ name ] or even: op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . MyColor

ops.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of OPs that match the patterns, relative to the inside of this OP . Multiple patterns may be provided. Numeric OP ids may also be used. The ops member is technically a Python Shortcut Object , not a true method. pattern - Can be string following the Pattern Matching rules, specifying which OPs to return, or an integer, which must be an OP Id. Multiple patterns can be given and all matched OPs will be returned. includeUtility - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, con

cook.description: Cook the contents of the operator if required. force - (Keyword, Optional) If True, the operator will always cook, even if it wouldn't under normal circumstances. recurse - (Keyword, Optional) If True, all children and sub-children of the operator will be cooked. includeUtility - (Keyword, Optional) If specified, controls whether or not utility components (eg Comments) are included in the results.

copyParameters.description: Copy all of the parameters from the specified operator . Both operators should be the same type. OP - The operator to copy. custom - (Keyword, Optional) When True, custom parameters will be copied. builtin - (Keyword, Optional) When True, built in parameters will be copied. op ( 'geo1' ) . copyParameters ( op ( 'geo2' ) )

changeType.description: Change referenced operator to a new operator type. After this call, this OP object should no longer be referenced. Instead use the returned OP object. OPtype - The python class name of the operator type you want to change this operator to. This is not a string, but instead is a class defined in the global td module . n = op ( 'wave1' ) . changeType ( nullCHOP ) #changes 'wave1' into a Null CHOP n = op ( 'text1' ) . changeType ( tcpipDAT ) #changes 'text1' operator into a TCPIP DAT

dependenciesTo.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of operator dependency paths between this operator and the specified operator. Multiple paths may be found.

evalExpression.description: Evaluate the expression from the context of this OP . Can be used to evaluate arbitrary snippets of code from arbitrary locations. str - The expression to evaluate. op ( 'wave1' ) . evalExpression ( 'me.digits' ) #returns 1 If the expression already resides in a parameter, use that parameters evalExpression() method instead.

destroy.description: Destroy the operator referenced by this OP . An exception will be raised if the OP 's operator has already been destroyed.

var.description: Evaluate a variable . This will return the empty string, if not found. Most information obtained from variables (except for Root and Component variables) are accessible through other means in Python, usually in the global td module . name - The variable name to search for. search - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True (which is default) the operator hierarchy is searched until a variable matching that name is found. If false, the search is constrained to the operator.

openMenu.description: Open a node menu for the operator at x, y. Opens at mouse if x & y are not specified. x - (Keyword, Optional) The X coordinate of the menu, measured in screen pixels. y - (Keyword, Optional) The Y coordinate of the menu, measured in screen pixels.

relativePath.description: Returns the relative path from this operator to the OP that is passed as the argument. See OP .shortcutPath for a version using expressions.

setInputs.description: Set all the operator inputs to the specified list. listOfOPs - A list containing one or more OPs. Entries in the list can be None to disconnect specific inputs. An empty list disconnects all inputs.

shortcutPath.description: Returns an expression from this operator to the OP that is passed as the argument. See OP .relativePath for a version using relative path constants. toParName - (Keyword, Optional) Return an expression to this parameter instead of its operator.

resetPars.description: Resets the specified parameters in the operator. Returns true if anything was changed. parNames (Keyword, Optional) - Specify parameters by Par name. parGroupNames (Keyword, Optional) - Specify parameters by ParGroup name. pageNames (Keyword, Optional) - Specify parameters by page name. includeBuiltin (Keyword, Optional) - Include builtin parameters. includeCustom (Keyword, Optional) - Include custom parameters. op ( 'player' ) . resetPars ( includeBuiltin = False ) # only reset custom

unload.description: Unloads CPU and GPU for the node. The memory will be realloted next time the node cooks, so make sure nothing is still using it to keep it released. cacheMemory - (Keyword, Optional) Currently only supported by the Movie File In TOP . If you are preloading into a Movie File In TOP that already has video, and the video format/resolution is the same, you can use the cacheMemory option to first unload the original movie and cache its memory, avoiding a reallocation when the preload() occurs. If Tru

asType.description: Casts this OP to the given type for editor code analysis. Returns the OP . opType - The type to cast this OP to. checkType: (Optional) If True, will check that this OP is of the given asType, and raise an exception if not.

addScriptError.description: Adds a script error to a node. msg - The error to add.

addError.description: Adds an error to an operator. Only valid if added while the operator is cooking. (Example Script SOP , CHOP , DAT ). msg - The error to add.

addWarning.description: Adds a warning to an operator. Only valid if added while the operator is cooking. (Example Script SOP , CHOP , DAT ). msg - The error to add.

errors.description: Get error messages associated with this OP . recurse - Get errors in any children or subchildren as well.

warnings.description: Get warning messages associated with this OP . recurse - Get warnings in any children or subchildren as well.

scriptErrors.description: Get script error messages associated with this OP . recurse - Get errors in any children or subchildren as well.

clearScriptErrors.description: Clear any errors generated during script execution. These may be generated during execution of DATs, Script Nodes, Replicator COMP callbacks, etc. recurse - Clear script errors in any children or subchildren as well. error - Pattern to match when clearing errors op ( '/project1' ) . clearScriptErrors ( recurse = True )

childrenCPUMemory.description: Returns the total CPU memory usage for all the children from this COMP .

childrenGPUMemory.description: Returns the total GPU memory usage for all the children from this COMP .

resetNodeSize.description: Reset the node tile size to its default width and height.

closeViewer.description: Close the floating content viewers of the OP . topMost - (Keyword, Optional) If True, any viewer window containing any parent of this OP is closed instead. op ( 'wave1' ) . closeViewer () op ( 'wave1' ) . closeViewer ( topMost = True ) # any viewer that contains 'wave1' will be closed.

openViewer.description: Open a floating content viewer for the OP . unique - (Keyword, Optional) If False, any existing viewer for this OP will be re-used and popped to the foreground. If unique is True, a new window is created each time instead. borders - (Keyword, Optional) If true, the floating window containing the viewer will have borders. op ( 'geo1' ) . openViewer ( unique = True , borders = False ) # opens a new borderless viewer window for 'geo1'

resetViewer.description: Reset the OP content viewer to default view settings. recurse - (Keyword, Optional) If True, this is done for all children and sub-children as well. op ( '/' ) . resetViewer ( recurse = True ) # reset the viewer for all operators in the entire file.

openParameters.description: Open a floating dialog containing the operator parameters.

fetch.description: Return an object from the OP storage dictionary. If the item is not found, and a default it supplied, it will be returned instead. key - The name of the entry to retrieve. default - (Optional) If provided and no item is found then the passed value/object is returned instead. storeDefault - (Keyword, Optional) If True, and the key is not found, the default is stored as well. search - (Keyword, Optional) If True, the parent of each OP is searched recursively until a match is found v = n . fetch (

fetchOwner.description: Return the operator which contains the stored key, or None if not found. key - The key to the stored entry you are looking for. who = n . fetchOwner ( 'sales5' ) #find the OP that has a storage entry called 'sales5'

store.description: Add the key/value pair to the OP 's storage dictionary, or replace it if it already exists. If this value is not intended to be saved and loaded in the toe file, it can be be given an alternate value for saving and loading, by using the method storeStartupValue described below. key - A string name for the storage entry. Use this name to retrieve the value using fetch(). value - The value/object to store. n . store ( 'sales5' , 34.5 ) # stores a floating point value 34.5. n . store ( 'moviebank'

unstore.description: For each key, remove it from the OP 's storage dictionary. Pattern Matching is supported as well. keys - The name or pattern defining which key/value pairs to remove from the storage dictionary. n . unstore ( 'sales*' ) # removes all entries from this OPs storage that start with 'sales'

storeStartupValue.description: Add the key/value pair to the OP 's storage startup dictionary. The storage element will take on this value when the file starts up. key - A string name for the storage startup entry. value - The startup value/object to store. n . storeStartupValue ( 'sales5' , 1 ) # 'sales5' will have a value of 1 when the file starts up.

unstoreStartupValue.description: For key, remove it from the OP 's storage startup dictionary. Pattern Matching is supported as well. This does not affect the stored value, just its startup value. keys - The name or pattern defining which key/value pairs to remove from the storage startup dictionary. n . unstoreStartupValue ( 'sales*' ) # removes all entries from this OPs storage startup that start with 'sales'

__getstate__.description: Returns a dictionary with persistent data about the object suitable for pickling and deep copies.

__setstate__.description: Reads the dictionary to update persistent details about the object, suitable for unpickling and deep copies.

ObjectCOMP.description: This class inherits from the COMP class. It is the parent class of these subclasses.

localTransform.description: The current local transform of the Object . This is the combination of both the parameters from the Xform and Pre-Xform page, without taking into account any parent or constraint transforms. See also Matrix Class , Position Class and Vector Class .

worldTransform.description: The current world transform of the Object .

transform.description: Gets the current transform of the Object as defined by the Translate, Rotate, Scale and Pivot parameters on the Xform page.

setTransform.description: Sets the Translate, Rotate, Scale and Pivot parameters on the Xform page from the given matrix . matrix - A matrix of values.

preTransform.description: Gets the current transform of the Object as defined by the Translate, Rotate, Scale and Pivot parameters on the Pre-Xform page.

setPreTransform.description: Sets the Translate, Rotate, Scale and Pivot parameters on the Pre-Xform page from the given matrix . matrix - A matrix of values.

relativeTransform.description: Returns a matrix that is the transform from the called object to the target object. This is, if you transform the called objects current transformation by the returned matrix, it will now be positioned at the target objects positions. target - The target ObjectCOMP . See also Matrix Class , Position Class and Vector Class .

Options.description: The Options class describes the set of configurable UI options. It can be accessed with the ui.options object.

resetToDefaults.description: Reset all options to their default values.

len.description: Returns the total number of options. a = len ( ui . options )

[].description: Get or set specific option given an option name key. v = ui . options [ ' DAT .width' ] ui . options [ ' DAT .width' ] = 50

Iterator.description: Iterate over each option name. for n in ui . options : print ( n ) # print the name of all options

Page.description: The Page Class describes the list of custom parameters contained on a page. Pages are created on components via the COMP Class. See also the guide Custom Parameters .

name.description: Get or set the name of the page.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

parGroups.description: A list of parameter groups on this page. A ParGroup is the set of parameters on one line.

pars.description: The list of parameters on this page.

index.description: The numeric index of this page.

isCustom.description: Boolean for whether this page is custom or not.

destroy.description: Destroy the page this object refers to, and all its parameters.

sort.description: Reorder custom parameter groups or parameters in specified order. n = op ( 'base1' ) page = n . appendCustomPage ( 'Custom1' ) page . appendFloat ( 'Value' ) page . appendFloat ( 'Speed' ) page . appendFloat ( 'Color' ) page . sort ( 'Speed' , 'Color' , 'Value' )

resetPars.description: Resets all the parameters in the page. Returns true if anything was changed. op ( 'geo1' ) . pages [ 0 ] . resetPars () op ( 'player' ) . customPages [ 'Setup' ] . resetPars ()

Pane.description: The Pane class describes an instance of a pane interface. It can be accessed through the ui.panes object. It is the parent class of the NetworkEditor Class .

owner.description: Get or set the component this pane points to.

link.description: Get or set the numeric link index.

enable.description: Get or set mouse and keyboard interactivity on the pane.

maximize.description: Enable or disable the pane maximize state.

name.description: Get or set the pane name.

ratio.description: Get or set the split proportion of the pane, if the pane was previously split.

bottomLeft.description: The coordinates of the bottom left corner, expressed as both x/y and u/v in a named tuple.

topRight.description: The coordinates of the top right corner, expressed as both x/y and u/v in a named tuple.

open.description: Returns True if the Pane is currently open.

type.description: The enumerated type of the pane. Example: NetworkEditor. The enumeration is called PaneType and consists of: PaneType.NETWORKEDITOR PaneType.PANEL PaneType.GEOMETRYVIEWER PaneType.TOPVIEWER PaneType.CHOPVIEWER PaneType.ANIMATIONEDITOR PaneType.PARAMETERS PaneType.TEXTPORT

changeType.description: Change the pane to the specified type. Will return a new Pane object that represents the Pane . After being called, the current Pane instance will no longer be valid. paneType - The type of pane to change this pane to. p = ui . panes [ 0 ] p = p . changeType ( PaneType . TOPVIEWER ) # note: must re-assign p to new object.

floatingCopy.description: Return a floating copy of the pane.

splitBottom.description: Split the bottom portion of the pane into a new pane.

splitLeft.description: Split the left portion of the pane into a new pane.

splitRight.description: Split the right portion of the pane into a new pane.

splitTop.description: Split the top portion of the pane into a new pane.

tearAway.description: Detach the pane into a floating window. Returns True if successful.

Panel.description: The Panel class manages Panel Components, and is used to access the state of a panel via its Panel Value Class .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs. In addition to the above, this object contains a member for each panel value in the component. a = op ( 'button1' ) . panel . u

PanelCOMP.description: The PanelCOMP describes an instance of a Panel Component . The state is represented by Panel Values . This class inherits from the COMP Class.

panel.description: The Panel from which Panel Values and the PanelValue Class may be accessed. (The second form is usually sufficient.) v = op ( 'button1' ) . panel . u . val v = op ( 'button1' ) . panel . u

panelRoot.description: The panelCOMP at the top of the panel hierarchy.

panelChildren.description: The children panelCOMPs of this operator.

x.description: The panel's x coordinate, as measured in pixels.

y.description: The panel's y coordinate, as measured in pixels.

width.description: The panel's width, as measured in pixels.

height.description: The panel's height, as measured in pixels.

marginX.description: The panel's x coordinate adjusted by margins as measured in pixels.

marginY.description: The panel's y coordinate adjusted by margins, as measured in pixels.

marginWidth.description: The panel's width adjusted by margins, as measured in pixels.

marginHeight.description: The panel's height adjusted by margins, as measured in pixels.

dropReady.description: The current state of the drop operation. True when the drop will be accepted.

panelParent.description: The nth panel parent of this operator. If n not specified, returns the panel parent. If n = 2, returns the parent of the parent, etc. If no panel parent exists at that level, None is returned. A panel parent is the panel wired to the input of this operator, or if that does not exist, the panel containing this operator. n - (Optional) n is the number of levels up to climb. When n = 1 it will return the operator's panel parent. p = me . panelParent ( 2 ) #grandfather

interactMouse.signature: interactMouse(u, v, leftClick=0, middleClick=0, rightClick=0, left=False, middle=False, right=False, wheel=0, pixels=False, Screen=False, quiet=True)
interactMouse.description: Simulates virtual mouse clicks, rollovers, moves and drags on a panel. It will also update the panel values: inside, insideu, insidev, state, u and v, in panels all the way up to the parent panel. The first (primary) mouse down/touch on a panel takes precedence over subsequent mouse downs and touches, and overrides any hovers. The primary state on a panel is global as each panel can only have one interaction updating its state. u - The first coordinate for the click to occur at. v - The second c

interactTouch.signature: interactTouch(u, v, hover='id', start='id', move='id', end='id', pixels=False, screen=False, quiet=True, aux='data')
interactTouch.description: Simulates virtual multiple touches and hovers on a panel via user assigned id. It will also update the panel values: inside, insideu, insidev, state, u and v, in panels all the way up to the parent panel. The first (primary) mouse down/touch on a panel takes precedence over subsequent mouse downs and touches, and overrides any hovers. The primary state on a panel is global. u - The first coordinate for the click to occur at. v - The second coordinate for the click to occur at. hover - (Keyword,

interactClear.description: Terminates any existing interactions. Touch or mouse interactions will end. Panels that are hovered over will end their rollover state.

interactStatus.description: Returns a list of panel interactions. Each interaction is encapsulated in a list as follows: [id, panel, state, primary]. id - The unique string identifying the interaction. '__MOUSE__' is used if the interaction was established via interactMouse. panel - The child panel being interacted with . state - Interaction types 'hover' or 'touch'. primary - True if the interaction is the first to act on the panel. op ( 'container1' ) . interactStatus # list the current hover over a slider and 2 touches

locateMouse.description: Returns a tuple containing the mouse coordinates relative to the Panel Component . If the mouse is not over a window containing the panel, None is returned instead.

locateMouseUV.description: Returns a tuple containing the normalized mouse coordinates relative to the Panel Component . If the mouse is not over a window containing the panel, None is returned instead.

setFocus.description: Set the hierarchical focus to this component, which sets the Panel value focusselect to 1. Focus will only be set successfully if the panel is open. moveMouse - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, the mouse will be moved to the component as well.

PanelValue.description: A PanelValue describes an instance to a Panel Value . They can be accessed through a component's panel member, and are used in the Panel Execute DAT .

name.description: The name of the panel value. See Panel Value for the list of possible names. name is a string.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

val.description: Get or set the panel value.

valid.description: True if the referenced panel value currently exists, False if it has been deleted.

Panes.description: The Panes class describes the list of all pane objects . It can be accessed from ui.panes .

createFloating.signature: createFloating(type=None, name=None, maxWidth=1920, maxHeight=1080, monitorSpanWidth=0.9, monitorSpanHeight=0.9)
createFloating.description: Return a floating pane. type - (Keyword, Optional) Type of pane created. See Pane for examples. Defaults to Network Editor . name - (Keyword, Optional) Name of the pane. This value can be used to find the pane in ui.panes. maxWidth - (Keyword, Optional) Upper limit on the width of the created window. Specified in pixels. maxHeight - (Keyword, Optional) Upper limit on the height of the created window. Specified in pixels. monitorSpanWidth - (Keyword, Optional) Specifies window width as a portion

len.description: Returns the total number of panes. a = len ( ui . panes )

[].description: Get specific pane, referenced by string or index. p = ui . panes [ 0 ] p = ui . panes [ 'pane1' ]

Iterator.description: Iterate over each pane. for n in ui . panes : # do something with n

Par.description: The Par class describes an instance of a single Parameter . See also Custom Parameters .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

val.description: Get or set the constant mode value of the parameter only. Important: To get the parameter's current working value, regardless of the Parameter Mode (constant, expression, export or bound), always use the eval () method described below . op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . val # the constant value op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . eval () # the evaluated parameter op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . val = 5 op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx = 5 # equivalent to above, more concise form op ( 'parexec1' ) . par . op = [ parent (), p

expr.description: Get or set the non-evaluated expression only. To get the parameter's current value, regardless of the Parameter Mode (constant, expression, export or bound), use the eval () method described below. op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . expr = ' absTime .frame' #set to match current frame When setting this member, the parameter will also be placed in expression mode. See mode member below. NOTE: For convenience, the expression is placed in double-quotes so you can safely put in expressions containing single

enable.description: Get or set the parameter's enable state. Can only be set on Custom Parameters . TouchDesigner 2023.30000+ If the parameter is in a ParGroup with multiple parameters, this will only get/set the enable state of this individual parameter. In previous versions of TouchDesigner, this got/set the enable state for the entire ParGroup .

enableExpr.description: Get or set an expression that controls the enable state for this parameter. This member is shared by all parameters and the parameter group. p . enableExpr = "me.par.X.menuIndex == 5" # Note the outside quotes, as this is an expression, not an object. TouchDesigner 2023.30000+ If the expression is a lambda function, it will be called once for each parameter in a parameter group, with the index as an argument... rgbaPar . enableExpr = "lambda parIndex: parIndex < 3" # enable first 3 elements rgba

readOnly.description: Get or set the parameter's read only status. When True the parameter cannot be modified through the UI, only scripting.

bindExpr.description: Get or set an expression that returns a bindable object. This can be used to bind this parameter's constant value to the referenced object's value. p . bindExpr = "op('geo1').par.tx" Note the outside quotes, as bindExpr is an expression, not an object.

bindMaster.description: The object to which this parameter is bound to, possibly None.

bindReferences.description: The (possibly empty) list of objects which bind to this parameter.

bindRange.description: Get or set parameter's range binding state. If True, min, max, clampMin, clampMax, normMin, normMax, normVal values will be based on master bind parameter. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

vecIndex.description: The parameter's vector index. For example, op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tz would have a value of 2.

name.description: Get or set the parameter's unique name. op ( 'myOperator' ) . par . Custompar . name = 'Translate' Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

label.description: Get or set the parameter's label. op ( 'myOperator' ) . par . Custompar . label = 'Translate' Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

subLabel.description: Returns the name of the sub-label.

startSection.description: Get or set the parameter's separator status. When True a visible separator is drawn between this parameter and the ones preceding it. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

help.description: Get or set a custom parameter's help text. To see any parameter's help, rollover the parameter while holding the Alt key. For built-in parameters this can be used to get the parameter's help text.

parGroup.description: The ParGroup of parameters this parameter belongs to. A ParGroup is a set of parameters sharing one line on a parameter dialog with a common label, example: Translate (x, y, z)..

min.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical minimum value. The parameter's value will be clamped at that minimum if clampMin = True. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

max.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical maximum value. The parameter's value will be clamped at that maximum if clampMax = True. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

clampMin.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical clamping behavior. If set to clampMin = True, the parameter will clamp on the lower end at the value specified in min Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

clampMax.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical clamping behavior. If set to clampMax = True, the parameter will clamp on the upper end at the value specified in max Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

normMin.description: Get or set the parameter's minimum slider value if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

normMax.description: Get or set the parameter's maximum slider value if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

normVal.description: Get or set the parameter's value as a normalized slider position. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

default.description: Get or set the parameter's default value. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

defaultBindExpr.description: Get or set the parameter's default bind expression. Can only be set on Custom Parameters. op ( 'base1' ) . par . Size . defaultBindExpr = 'me.par.tx'

defaultExpr.description: Get or set the parameter's default expression. Can only be set on Custom Parameters . # value defaults to this expression. op ( 'base1' ) . par . Size . defaultExpr = 'me.time.frame'

defaultMode.description: Get or set the parameter's default evaluation mode. op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . defaultMode = ParMode . EXPRESSION The mode is one of: ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.EXPRESSION, or ParMode.EXPORT, or ParMode.BIND. See Parameter_Dialog#Working_with_Parameter_Modes for more information.

order.description: Get or set the parameter's position on the parameter page. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

page.description: Get or set the parameter page the custom parameter is part of. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

sequence.description: The Sequence this parameter belongs to. None if not in a sequence.

sequenceBlock.description: The SequenceBlock this parameter belongs to. None if not in a sequence.

password.description: Get or set the parameter's password mode. When True all text is rendered as asterisks. Can only be set on Custom string, int or float parameters. Custom Parameters .

mode.description: Get or set the parameter's evaluation mode. op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . mode = ParMode . EXPRESSION The mode is one of: ParMode.CONSTANT , ParMode.EXPRESSION , or ParMode.EXPORT , or ParMode.BIND . See Parameter_Dialog#Working_with_Parameter_Modes for more information.

menuNames.description: Get or set a list of all possible menu choice names. In the case of non menu parameters, None is returned. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

menuLabels.description: Get or set a list of all possible menu choice labels. In the case of non menu parameters, None is returned. Can only be set on Custom Parameters .

menuIndex.description: Get or set a menu constant value by its index.

menuSource.description: Get or set an expression that returns an object with .menuItems .menuNames members. This can be used to create a custom menu whose entries dynamically follow that of another menu for example. Simple menu sources include another parameter with a menu c, an object created by tdu.TableMenu , or an object created by TDFunctions.parMenu . p . menuSource = "op('audiodevin1').par.device" Note the outside quotes, as menuSource is an expression, not an object.

exportOP.description: The operator exporting to this parameter.

exportSource.description: The object exporting to this parameter. Examples: Cell , Channel or None.

collapser.description: Returns True if the parameter is a parent of collapsable parameters (ie. a collapser).

collapsable.description: Returns True if the parameter is collapsable.

styleCloneImmune.description: Get or set the parameter's style clone immunity. When False , the parameter definition is matched to any matching master parameter its operator is cloned to. When True , it is left unchanged.

lastScriptChange.description: Return information about when this parameter was last modified by a script. Cleared when the parameter is updated via the UI. python >>> op ( '/level1' ) . par . invert . lastScriptChange SetInfo ( dat = type : textDAT path : / text1 , function = '<module>' , line = 1 , frame = 300061 , timeStamp = 1613150878 )

isDefault.description: True when the parameter value, expression and mode are in their default settings.

isFloat.description: True for floating point numeric parameters.

valid.description: True if the referenced parameter currently exists, False if it has been deleted.

index.description: A unique identifier for the parameter. May change in the case of custom parameters.

style.description: Describes the behavior and contents of the custom parameter. Example 'Float' , 'Int' , ' Pulse ' , 'XYZ' , etc.

hidden.description: Get the parameter's hidden status. When True the parameter is considered obsolete or irrelevant and should not be modified. They are not shown in the dialog but only maintained for backward compatibility.

eval.description: Evaluate a parameter. This value may be derived by the parameter's constant value, expression, export, or bind, dependent on its mode. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . eval () Calling eval on an OP parameter that can hold multiple OPs will return a single OP if there is only 1 result, a list of OPs if there are more than 1, and None if there are no results.

evalNorm.description: Similar to eval() but the returns the normalized slider value.

evalExpression.description: Evaluate the expression portion of a parameter, if it contains one. This will ignore any exports, etc. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . evalExpression () Note : the results of evalExpression is always the expression's Python return value, which can be slightly different than Par.eval() . For example, in parameters that hold an operator, .eval() will always return an operator if it exists, even if the expression actually returns a string path. The evalExpression function would return the string pat

evalExport.description: Evaluate the export portion of a parameter, if it contains one. This will ignore any expressions, etc. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . evalExport ()

evalOPs.description: Evaluate the parameter as series of operators. This is useful for a custom parameter that specifies a list of operator paths for example. a = op ( 'base1' ) . par . Paths . evalOPs ()

evalFile.description: Evaluate the parameter as a file path. This returns a FileInfo object with the full path. a = op ( 'moviefilein1' ) . par . file . evalFile () print ( a . ext ) print ( a . baseName ) print ( a . exists )

pulse.description: Pulsing sets a parameter to the specific value, cooks the operator, then restores the parameter to its previous value. For pulse type parameters no value or time is specified or used. value - (Optional) The value to pulse this parameter with, default is 1. frames - (Optional) Number of frames before restoring the parameter to its original value. seconds - (Optional) Number of seconds before restoring the parameter to its original value. op ( 'moviein1' ) . par . reload . pulse ( 1 ) #set the rel

reset.description: Resets the parameter to its default state. Returns true if anything was changed. op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . reset ()

copy.description: Copy the specified parameter . Par - The parameter to copy. op ( 'geo1' ) . par . tx . copy ( op ( 'geo2' ) . par . tx )

destroy.description: Destroy the parameter referenced by this Par. An exception will be raised if the parameter has already been destroyed. Only custom and sequential parameters can be destroyed. Destroying a sequential parameter will destroy its entire block. Note: When any parameter is destroyed, any existing parameter objects will be invalid and should be re-fetched.

isSamePar.description: True if the provided Par is the same parameter on the same operator. Because op('container1').par.x == op('container2').par.x compares values and op('container1').par.x is op('container1').par.x is always False (because of TouchDesigner internals), you must use isSamePar to compare parameter objects. Similarly op('container2').par.Myfloat in op('container2').customPars will return unreliable results. par - The parameter to compare identity with.

ParCollection.description: The ParCollection class can be used to access Parameters . To access a parameter you need to use its internal name, which you can obtain by hovering your mouse over the parameter name, and looking at the popup that will come up. See also Par Class . An operator's instance of this can be found in OP ...

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

[].description: Parameters may be easily accessed using the [] subscript and assignment operators. name - Must be an exact string name. Wildcards are not supported. If not found None is returned. p = op ( 'base1' ) . par [ 'Myfloat5' ]

ParGroup.description: The ParGroup class describes an instance of a single ParGroup .

bindExpr.description: Get or set expressions that return an object to bind to. This can be used to bind this parameter's constant values to the referenced object's value. Example: p . bindExpr = ( "op('geo1').par.tx" , "op('geo1').par.ty" , "op('geo1').par.tz" ) Note the outside quotes, as bindExpr is an expression, not an object.

bindMaster.description: The objects to which this parameter is bound to, possibly None.

bindRange.description: Get or set parameter's range binding state. If True, min, max, clampMin, clampMax, normMin, normMax, normVal values will be based on master bind parameter. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

bindReferences.description: The (possibly empty) lists of objects which bind to this parameter.

clampMax.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical clamping behaviors. If set to clampMax = True , the parameter will clamp on the upper end at the value specified in max Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

clampMin.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical clamping behaviors. If set to clampMin = True , the parameter will clamp on the lower end at the value specified in min Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

collapsable.description: Returns True if the parameter is collapsable.

collapser.description: Returns True if the parameter is a parent of collapsable parameters (ie. a collapser).

default.description: Get or set the parameter's default values. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

defaultExpr.description: Get or set the parameter's default expressions. Can only be set on Custom Parameters. # value defaults to this expression. op ( 'base1' ) . parGroup . Size . defaultExpr = ( 'me.time.frame' , 'me.time.frame' , 'me.time.frame' )

defaultBindExpr.description: Get or set the parameter's default bind expressions. Can only be set on Custom Parameters. # value defaults to this expression. op ( 'base1' ) . parGroup . Size . defaultBindExpr = ( 'me.par.tx' , 'me.par.ty' , 'me.par.tz' )

defaultMode.description: Get or set the parameter's evaluation modes. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . defaultMode = ( ParMode . EXPRESSION , ParMode . EXPRESSION , ParMode . EXPRESSION ) The modes are one of: ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.EXPRESSION, or ParMode.EXPORT, or ParMode.BIND. See Parameter_Dialog#Working_with_Parameter_Modes for more information.

enable.description: Get or set the parameter group's enable state. Can only be set on Custom Parameters . This will also set the enable member of all individual parameters in the group.

enableExpr.description: Get or set an expression that controls the enable state for this parameter group. This member is shared by all parameters and the parameter group. p . enableExpr = "me.par.X.menuIndex == 5" # Note the outside quotes, as this is an expression, not an object. TouchDesigner 2023.30000+ If the expression is a lambda function, it will be called once for each parameter in the parameter group, with the index as an argument... rgbaPar . enableExpr = "lambda parIndex: parIndex < 3" # enable first 3 eleme

exportOP.description: The operators exporting to this parameter.

exportSource.description: tuple of objects exporting to this parameter. Examples: Cell, Channel or None.

expr.description: Get or set the non-evaluated expressions only. To get the parameter's current values, regardless of the Parameter Mode (constant, expression, export or bound), use the eval() method described below. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . expr = ( ' absTime .frame' , ' absTime .frame' , ' absTime .frame' ) # set to match current frame When setting this member, the parameter will also be placed in expression mode. See mode member below. NOTE: For convenience, the expression is placed in double-quotes so y

help.description: Get or set a custom parameter's help text. To see any parameter's help, rollover the parameter while holding the Alt key.

isDefault.description: True when the parameter value, expression and mode are in their default settings.

isFloat.description: True for floating point numeric parameters.

label.description: Get or set the parameter's label. op ( 'myOperator' ) . parGroup . Custompar . label = 'Translate' Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

max.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical maximum values. The parameter's values will be clamped at that maximum if clampMax = True . Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

menuIndex.description: Get or set a tuple of menu constant values by their indices.

menuLabels.description: Get or set a tuple of lists of all possible menu choice labels. In the case of non menu parameters, None(s) are returned. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

menuNames.description: Get or set a tuple of lists of all possible menu choice names. In the case of non menu parameters, None(s) are returned. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

menuSource.description: Get or set a tuple of expressions that returns objects with .menuItems .menuNames members. This can be used to create a custom menu whose entries dynamically follow that of another menu for example.

min.description: Get or set the parameter's numerical minimum values. The parameter's values will be clamped at that minimum if clampMin = True for the particular Par. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

mode.description: Get or set the parameter's evaluation modes. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . mode = ( ParMode . EXPRESSION , ParMode . EXPRESSION , ParMode . EXPRESSION ) The modes are one of: ParMode.CONSTANT , ParMode.EXPRESSION , or ParMode.EXPORT , or ParMode.BIND . See Parameter_Dialog#Working_with_Parameter_Modes for more information.

name.description: Get or set the parameter's unique name. op ( 'myOperator' ) . parGroup . Custompar . name = 'Translate' Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

baseName.description: Get or set the parameter's base name. This excludes any sequence prefixes, sequence indices or name suffixes. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

normMax.description: Get or set the parameter's maximum slider values if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

normMin.description: Get or set the parameter's minimum slider values if the parameter is a numerical slider. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

normVal.description: Get or set the parameter's values as a normalized slider position. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

order.description: Get or set the parameter's position on the parameter page. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

page.description: Get or set the parameter page the custom parameter is part of. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

password.description: Get or set the parameter's password mode. When True all text is rendered as asterisks. Can only be set on Custom string, int or float parameters. Custom Parameters.

readOnly.description: Get or set the parameter's read only status. When True the parameter cannot be modified through the UI, only scripting.

startSection.description: Get or set the parameter's separator status. When True a visible separator is drawn between this parameter and the ones preceding it. Can only be set on Custom Parameters.

style.description: Describes the behavior and contents of the custom parameter. Example 'Float', 'Int', ' Pulse ', 'XYZ', etc.

subLabel.description: Returns the names of the sub-label.

val.description: Get or set the constant values of the parameter only. To get the parameter's current values, regardless of the Parameter Modes ( constant , expression , export or bound ), use the eval() method described below. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . val # the constant values op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . eval () # the evaluated parameter op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . val = ( 1 , 2 , 3 ) op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t = ( 1 , 2 , 3 ) #equivalent to above, more concise form When setting this member, the p

valid.description: True if the referenced parameter currently exists, False if it has been deleted.

index.description: The parameter's order in the list.

sequence.description: The Sequence this parGroup is a part of. None if not in a sequence.

sequenceBlock.description: The SequenceBlock this parGroup belongs to. None if not in a sequence.

blockIndex.description: The index of the parGroup within its SequenceBlock . None if not in a sequence.

sequenceIndex.description: The index of the parGroup's SequenceBlock in its Sequence . None if not in a sequence.

size.description: Get or set the parameter's vector size.

maxSize.description: Returns the maximum number of Par elements for this ParGroup

defaultSize.description: Returns the default number of Par elements for this ParGroup .

suffixes.description: Returns a list of suffixes used to name the Par elements of this ParGroup .

copy.description: Copy the specified parameter. ParGroup - The parameter to copy. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . copy ( op ( 'geo2' ) . parGroup . t )

destroy.description: Destroy the parameter referenced by this ParGroup . An exception will be raised if the parameter has already been destroyed. Only custom and sequential parameters can be destroyed. Destroying a sequential parameter will destroy its entire block. Note: When any parameter is destroyed, any existing parameter objects will be invalid and should be re-fetched.

eval.description: Evaluate a parameter group. This value may be derived by the parameter group's constant value, expression, or export, dependent on its mode. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . eval ()

evalExport.description: Evaluate the export portions of a parameter, if it contains any. This will ignore any expressions, etc. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . evalExport ()

evalExpression.description: Evaluate the expression portions of a parameter, if it contains any. This will ignore any exports, etc. a = op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . evalExpression () To evaluate an arbitrary expression string, that is not inside a parameter, see OP.evalExpression .

evalNorm.description: Similar to eval() but the returns the normalized slider values.

evalOPs.description: Evaluate each parameter as a series of operators. This is useful for a custom parameter that specifies a list of operator paths for example. a = op ( 'base1' ) . parGroup . Paths . evalOPs ()

pars.description: Returns a (possibly empty) list of parameter objects that match the pattern. pattern - Is a string following the Pattern Matching rules, specifying which parameters to return. # translate parameters newlist = op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . pars ( 't?' )

reset.description: Resets the parameter group to its default state. Returns true if anything was changed. op ( 'geo1' ) . parGroup . t . reset ()

isSameParGroup.description: True if the provided parGroup is the same ParGroup on the same operator. Because op('container1').parGroup.x == op('container2').parGroup.x compares values and op('container1').parGroup.x is op('container1').parGroup.x is always False (because of TouchDesigner internals), you must use isParGroup to compare ParGroup objects. Similarly op('container2').par.Myfloat in op('container2').customPars will return unreliable results. parGroup - The ParGroup to compare identity with.

ParGroupCollection.description: The ParGroupCollection class can be used to access ParGroups . To access a parGroup you need to use its internal name, which you can obtain by hovering your mouse over the parameter name, and looking at the popup that will come up. See also ParGroup Class . An operator's instance of this can be foun...

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

[].description: ParGroups may be easily accessed using the [] subscript and assignment operators. name - Must be an exact string name. Wildcards are not supported. If not found None is returned. p = op ( 'base1' ) . parGroup [ 'Myfloat5' ]

ParGroupPulse.description: The ParGroupPulse class describes a subclass of a ParGroup ending with a pulse parameter. See also Custom ParGroup .

pulse.description: Pulsing sets a parameter to the specific value, cooks the operator, then restores the parameter to its previous value. For pulse type parameters no value or time is specified or used. value - (Optional) The tuple to pulse this parGroup with, default is [ 1 ] . frames - (Optional) Number of frames before restoring the parameter to its original value. seconds - (Optional) Number of seconds before restoring the parameter to its original value. op ( 'moviein1' ) . parGroup . reload . pulse ([ 1 ]) #

ParGroupUnit.description: The ParGroupUnit class describes a subclass of a ParGroup ending with a unit parameter. See also Custom ParGroup .

unit.description: The unit parameter in this ParGroupUnit object.

unitPar.description: The unit parameter in this ParGroupUnit object.

Peer.description: A Peer describes the network connection originating a message in the callback functions found in oscinDAT , tcpipDAT , udpinDAT , udtinDAT .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

port.description: The network port associated with the peer.

address.description: The network address associated with the peer.

hostname.description: The network hostname associated with the peer.

close.description: Close the peer connection. Returns True if successful. Closing a peer can be useful when implementing HTML server protocols for example.

sendBytes.description: Send a sequence of bytes through this connection. No terminators are appended. Note: the message will not be OSC formatted. Messages can any combination of strings, byte arrays, or individual single-byte numeric values. To serialize non-byte values (example floats or integers) there are several python modules to do this, such as pickle or struct. Returns the number of bytes sent. n . sendBytes ( 'TYPE' , 23 , 255 , 12 , 0x34 , b ' \x01\x00\x02\x00\x03\x00\x00\x00 ' )

sendOSC.description: Send an OSC formatted message through this connection. Multiple pairs of address/valueLists arguments to be passed. This method will automatically decide what OSC type to convert the passed values to. Returns the number of bytes sent. address - The OSC address to label this message with. values - A list of values to send in this message. asBundle - (Keyword, Optional) When true, all messages are sent inside a single bundle. useNonStandardTypes - (Keyword, Optional) When True, values may be sent

send.description: Send a sequence of strings through this connection. Note: the message will not be OSC formatted. messsage - One or more strings to send across the network connection. terminator - (Keyword, Optional) Specifies how the message is to be terminated. If no append terminator is specified, a null character will automatically be appended to the message. To send no terminator, use terminator=''. Returns the number of bytes sent. n . send ( 'Hello' , 'World' , terminator = ' \r\n ' ) # send two strings w
