Point.description: A Point describes an instance to a single geometry point . They are accessible through the SOP.points member.

index.description: The point index in the list.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

P.description: The coordinates as AttributeData . Individual components can be read or written with the [] operator. point . P [ 0 ] = 5 point . P = ( 1 , 0 , 1 )

x.description: Get or set x coordinate value. This is the same as P[0].

y.description: Get or set y coordinate value. This is the same as P[1].

z.description: Get or set z coordinate value. This is the same as P[2].

normP.description: The normalized position of this point within the bounding box of the SOP . Will always be in the range [0,1]. Expressed as tdu.Position object.

destroy.description: Destroy and remove the actual point this object refers to. This operation is only valid when the primitive belongs to a scriptSOP . Note: after this call, other existing Point objects in this SOP may no longer be valid.

Points.description: The Points class describes the set of point objects owned by one SOP .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

len.description: Returns the total number of points. a = len ( op ( 'box1' ) . points )

[].description: Get a specific point given an integer index. n = op ( 'box1' ) . points [ 0 ]

Iterator.description: Iterate over each point. for m in op ( 'box1' ) . points : # do something with m, which is a Point

Poly.description: A Poly describes an instance of a single geometry polygon . It is an instance of a Prim Class .

closed.description: Returns True if the poly is closed, False otherwise.

Position.description: The position class holds a single 3 component position. A position is a single point in space, and it's important to use a position or vector as appropriate for the data that is being calculated, since matrix operations on them will end in different results. When being multiplied by a Matrix , this ...

x.description: Gets or sets the X component of the position.

y.description: Gets or sets the Y component of the position.

z.description: Gets or sets the Z component of the position.

translate.description: Translates the position by the specified values. x, y, z - The values to translate by. p . translate ( 5 , 2 , 0 )

scale.description: Scales each component of the position by the specified values. x, y, z - The values to scale each component of the position by. p . scale ( 1 , 2 , 1 )

copy.description: Returns a new position that is a copy of the position. newV = v . copy ()

Preferences.description: The Preferences class describes the set of configurable preferences that are retained between sessions. It can be accessed with the ui.preferences object or through the Preferences Dialog .

defaults.description: A dictionary of preferences with their default values.

save.description: Save preference values to disk. Unless saved, changes to preferences will be lost, next time application is started.

resetToDefaults.description: Reset all preferences to their default values.

len.description: Returns the total number of preferences. a = len ( ui . preferences )

[].description: Get or set specific preference given a preference name key. v = ui . preferences [ 'dats.autoindent' ] ui . preferences [ 'dats.autoindent' ] = 0

Iterator.description: Iterate over each preference name. for p in ui . preferences : print ( p ) # print the name of all preferences

Prim.description: A Prim describes an instance to a single geometry primitive . They are accessible through the SOP.prims member.

center.description: Get or set the barycentric coordinate of this primitive. It is expressed as a TDU.Position object.

index.description: The primitive index in the list.

normal.description: The calculated normal vector of this primitive, expressed as a TDU.Vector object.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

weight.description: The associated weight of the primitive. Only certain primitives, such as those created by the Metaball SOP can modify this value from its default of 2.0.

direction.description: A normalized vector pointing from the centroid of the SOP to the centroid of this primitive.

min.description: The minimum coordinates of this primitive along each dimension, expressed as a TDU.Position object.

max.description: The maximum coordinates of this primitive along each dimension, expressed as a TDU.Position object.

size.description: The size of this primitive along each dimension, expressed as a TDU.Position object.

destroy.description: Destroy and remove the actual primitive this object refers to. This operation is only valid when the primitive belongs to a scriptSOP . Note: after this call, other existing Prim objects in this SOP may no longer be valid. destroyPoints - (Keyword, Optional) If True, its points are destroyed as well, if false, they are simply detached. The argument is True by default.

eval.description: Evaluate the position on the primitive given the u,v coordinates. u,v should be in the range [0,1]. Note: Polygons and curves ignore the v parameter. center = op ( 'box1' ) . prim [ 0 ] . eval ( 0.5 , 0.5 )

len.description: Returns the total number of vertices. a = len ( op ( 'box1' ) . prim [ 0 ])

[].description: Get specific vertex from a Mesh given integer row and column values. v = op ( 'grid1' ) . prims [ 2 , 3 ]

Iterator.description: Iterate over each vertex. for m in op ( 'box1' ) . prims [ 5 ]: # do something with m, which is a Vertex

Prims.description: The Prims class describes the set of prim objects (primitives) owned by one SOP .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

len.description: Returns the total number of prims. a = len ( op ( 'box1' ) . prims )

[].description: Get a specific prim given an integer index. n = op ( 'box1' ) . prims [ 0 ]

Iterator.description: Iterate over each prim. for m in op ( 'box1' ) . prims : # do something with m, which is a Prim

ProductEntry.description: A class to interact with a dongle entry for a single dongle connected to the system.

licenseType.description: Returns the license type for this product entry on the dongle.

updateDate.description: The date the product entry is valid until. Returns a tuple in the form (YYYY, MM, DD).

version.description: The version of TouchDesigner this dongle product entry is valid for.

Project.description: The Project class describes the current session. It can be accessed with the project object, found in the automatically imported td module . Members changed in this such as the 'paths' member will be written to disk when the project is saved.

folder.description: The folder at which the project resides.

name.description: The filename under which the project is saved.

saveVersion.description: The App version number when the project was last saved.

saveBuild.description: The App build number when the project was last saved.

saveTime.description: The time and date the project was last saved.

saveOSName.description: The App operating system name when the project was last saved.

saveOSVersion.description: The App operating system version when the project was last saved.

paths.description: A dictionary which can be used to define URL-syntax path prefixes, enabling you to move your media to different locations easily. This dictionary is saved and loaded in the .toe file. Example: Run project.paths['movies'] = 'C:/MyMovies' , and reference it with a parameter expression: movies://butterfly.jpg . To manually convert between expanded and collapsed paths, use tdu.collapsePath() and tdu.expandPath , for example tdu.expandPath('movies://butterfly.jpg') expands to C:/MyMovies/butterfly.jp

cookRate.description: Get or set the maximum number of frames processed each second. In general you should not need to use this. It is preferred to look at the FPS of the root component to know the cooking rate. Individual components may have their own rates, specified by rate. a = project . cookRate # get the current cook rate project . cookRate = 30 # set the cook rate to 30 FPS Note: This is displayed and set in the user interface at the bottom-left: the " FPS " field.

realTime.description: Get or set the real time cooking state. When True, frames may be skipped in order to maintain the cookRate. When False, all frames are processed sequentially regardless of duration. This is useful to render movies out using the Movie File Out TOP without dropping any frames for example. a = project . realTime project . realTime = False # turn off real time playback.

isPrivate.description: True when the project networks cannot be directly viewed.

isPrivateKey.description: True when the private networks are accessible by a pass phrase.

cacheParameters.description: Cache parameter values instead of always evaluating.

externalToxModifiedInProject.description: Callback for when an external tox has been modified in the current project and there are other instances of the same tox loaded elsewhere in the project.

externalToxModifiedOnDisk.description: Callback for when an external tox file has been modified on disk.

windowOnTop.description: Get or set the window on top state.

windowStartMode.description: Get or set the window start mode. The mode is one of: WindowStartMode.AUTO , WindowStartMode.FULL , WindowStartMode.LEFT , WindowStartMode.RIGHT or WindowStartMode.CUSTOM .

windowDraw.description: Get or set the window drawing state.

windowStartCustomWidth.description: Get or set the window start width. Only used when windowStartMode is WindowStartMode.CUSTOM .

windowStartCustomHeight.description: Get or set the window start height. Only used when windowStartMode is WindowStartMode.CUSTOM .

windowStartCustomX.description: Get or set the window start X position. Only used when windowStartMode is WindowStartMode.CUSTOM .

windowStartCustomY.description: Get or set the window start Y position. Only used when windowStartMode is WindowStartMode.CUSTOM .

performOnStart.description: Get or set the perform on start state.

performWindowPath.description: Get or set the perform window path.

resetAudioOnDeviceChange.description: Get or set whether audio devices momentarily reset when devices are added or removed to the system.

sdrReferenceWhiteNits.description: Get or set Reference White brightness in nits, of SDR colors/content.

hdrReferenceWhiteNits.description: Get or set Reference White brightness in nits, of HDR colors/content.

editorWindowPixelFormat.description: Get or set the pixel format the editor window will use. This is not the format Window COMPs or the Perform window will use (which is set with a Window COMP ). The project must be saved and TouchDesigner must be restarted for this to take affect. Valid values are from the WindowPixelFormat enum: WindowPixelFormat.SDR8_FIXED WindowPixelFormat.SDR10_FIXED WindowPixelFormat.HDR10_FIXED WindowPixelFormat.HDR16_FLOAT

workingColorSpace.description: Get or set the working color space used for this project. The project must be saved and TouchDesigner must be restarted for this to take affect. Valid values are from the WorkingColorSpace enum: WorkingColorSpace.PASS_THROUGH WorkingColorSpace.ACES_CG WorkingColorSpace.SRGB_LINEAR

load.description: Load a specific .toe file from disk. path - (Optional) The path of the file to load. If not specified, loads the default .toe file , as specified in preferences. project . load ( 'test_demo .toe ' )

save.description: Save the current session to disk. Returns True if a file was saved, False otherwise (eg, if the file exists, and when prompted, the user selects to not overwrite). path - (Optional) If not provided the default/current filename is incremented and used. The current file is project.name under folder project.folder. saveExternalToxs - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, will save out the contents of any COMP that references an external .tox into the referenced .tox file. project . save ( 'test_demo

quit.description: Quit the project. force - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, unsaved changes will be discarded without prompting. crash - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, the application will terminate unexpectedly. This is used for system testing. project . quit () #quit project, possibly prompting for unsaved changes if 'Prompt to Save on Exit' in Preferences dialog is enabled. project . quit ( force = True ) #quit project immediately.

addPrivacy.description: Add privacy to a toe file with the given key. Privacy can only be added to toes that currently have no privacy, and are using a Pro license. key - The key phrase. This should resolve to a non-blank string. project . addPrivacy ( 'secret' )

removePrivacy.description: Completely remove privacy from a toe file. key - The current privacy key phrase. project . removePrivacy ( 'secret' )

accessPrivateContents.description: Gain access to a private file. The file will still be private the next time it is saved or re-opened. key - The current privacy key phrase. project . accessPrivateContents ( 'secret' )

applyWindowSettings.description: Applies the project's window start settings to the current TouchDesigner window.

stack.description: Formatted contents of current cook and parameter evaluation stack. print ( project . stack ())

pythonStack.description: Formatted contents of current python stack. print ( project . pythonStack ())

Quaternion.description: Holds a Quaternion object which can be used to manipulate rotations in various ways.

x.description: Get or set the x component of the quaternion.

y.description: Get or set the y component of the quaternion.

z.description: Get or set the z component of the quaternion.

w.description: Get or set the w component of the quaternion.

lerp.description: Returns the linear interpolation of the quaternion with another quaternion and an interpolation factor. The quaternion argument can be anything from which a quaternion can be derived ie. (x,y,z,w), Matrix, etc. The interpolation factor must be between 0 and 1. q3 = q . lerp ( q2 , factor )

length.description: Returns the length of the quaternion. l = q . length ()

cross.description: Returns the cross product of the quaternion and argument. The quaternion argument can be anything from which a quaternion can be derived ie. (x,y,z,w), Matrix, etc. l = q . cross ( q2 )

rotate.description: Rotates a vector using the current quaternion. Returns a new vector. v2 = q . rotate ( v1 )

slerp.description: Returns the spherical interpolation of the quaternion with another quaternion and an interpolation factor. The quaternion argument can be anything from which a quaternion can be derived ie. (x,y,z,w), Matrix, etc. q3 = q . slerp ( q2 , factor )

eulerAngles.description: Returns euler angles in degrees as a tuple (i.e. pitch as x, yaw as y, roll as z) from current quaternion and a rotation order. The 'order' argument can be set to any valid rotation order which by default is set to 'xyz'. r = q . eulerAngles ( order = 'xyz' )

fromEuler.description: Returns and set the current quaternion from euler angles in degrees as a 3 inputs argument (i.e. pitch as x, yaw as y, roll as z). The 'order' argument can be set to any valid rotation order which by default is set to 'xyz'. r = q . fromEuler ( order = 'xyz' )

axis.description: Returns the rotation axis vector of the quaternion. v = q . axis ()

dot.description: Returns the dot product of the quaternion and the argument. The quaternion argument can be anything from which a quaternion can be derived ie. (x,y,z,w), Matrix, etc. l = q . dot ( q2 )

exp.description: Returns the exponential of the quaternion as a new quaternion. q2 = q . exp ()

copy.description: Creates a copy of the quaternion with separate values.

log.description: Returns the natural logarithm of the current quaternion as a new quaternion. l = q . log ()

inverse.description: Invert the quaternion in place. q . inverse ()

angle.description: Returns the rotation angle (in degrees) of the quaternion. a = q . angle ()

Quaternion.description: Applies the rotation of one quaternion to another quaternion. # apply rotation of q2 to q1 q1 *= q2

RampTOP.description: This class inherits from the TOP class . It references a specific Ramp TOP .

Run.description: The Run class describes a single instance of a delayed script execution. See Run Command Examples for more info. They can be accessed from the runs object. Scripts can be executed with delays with the following methods:

active.description: Get or set whether or not this script will execute once its target frame is reached.

group.description: Get or set the group label associated with this script.

isCell.description: Returns true when the source is a cell , from a Cell.run() call.

isDAT.description: Returns true when the source is a DAT , from a DAT .run() call.

isString.description: Returns true when the source is a string, from a td module run() call

path.description: The operator location from which this script will execute.

remainingFrames.description: Get or set the remaining number of frames before the execution will occur.

remainingMilliseconds.description: Get or set the remaining number of milliseconds before the execution will occur.

source.description: The source of the run. It will be either a DAT , cell , or string.

kill.description: Kill this run before it executes, and remove it from the global runs list, located in the td Module .

Runs.description: The Runs class describes the set of all delayed run objects . It can be accessed with the runs object, found in the automatically imported td module . See Run Command Examples for more info.

SOP.description: A SOP describes a reference to a SOP operator, containing points and primitives .

points.description: The set of points contained in this SOP .

prims.description: The set of primitives contained in this SOP .

numPoints.description: The number of points contained in this SOP .

numVertices.description: The number of vertices contained in all primitives within this SOP .

numPrims.description: The number of primitivies contained in this SOP .

pointAttribs.description: The set of point attributes defined in this SOP .

primAttribs.description: The set of primitive attributes defined in this SOP .

vertexAttribs.description: The set of vertex attributes defined in this SOP .

pointGroups.description: Returns a dictionary of point groups defined for this SOP .

primGroups.description: Returns a dictionary of primitive groups defined for this SOP .

center.description: Get or set the barycentric coordinate of this operator's geometry. It is expressed as a Position .

min.description: The minimum coordinates of this operator's geometry along each dimension, expressed as a Position .

max.description: The maximum coordinates of this operator's geometry along each dimension, expressed as Position .

size.description: The size of this operator's geometry along each dimension, expressed as a Position .

bounds.description: Returns an object with the bounds, center and size of the SOP 's geometry. Note: This method replaces the deprecated computeBounds method. The method works the same, but the keywords now default to false. display - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, only SOP operators whose display flag are turned on are included in the calculation. When False, the states of the display flag is ignored. render - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, only SOP operators whose render flag are turned on are included

save.description: Saves the geometry to the file system. Multiple file types are supported. Returns the filename and path saved. filepath - (Optional) The path and filename to save to. If not given then a default filename will be used, and the file will be saved in the project.folder folder. createFolders - (Keyword, Optional) If True, it creates the not existent directories provided by the filepath. name = n . save () #save in native format with default name. n . save ( 'output.bgeo' ) #alternate format compatib

Segment.description: A Segment object describes a single segment from a Timer CHOP .

beginFrames.description: The beginning point of the segment expressed in frames.

beginSamples.description: The beginning point of the segment expressed in samples.

beginSeconds.description: The beginning point of the segment expressed in seconds.

custom.description: dictionary of all the extra column values associated with the segment.

cycle.description: Whether or not the segment will repeat itself.

cycleEndAlertFrames.description: The amount of time before cycling the callback will be executed, expressed in frames.

cycleEndAlertSamples.description: The amount of time before cycling the callback will be executed, expressed in samples.

cycleEndAlertSeconds.description: The amount of time before cycling the callback will be executed, expressed in seconds.

cycleLimit.description: Whether or not the segment will repeat itself indefinitely.

delayFrames.description: The delay portion of the segment expressed in frames.

delaySamples.description: The delay portion of the segment expressed in samples.

delaySeconds.description: The delay portion of the segment expressed in seconds.

lengthFrames.description: The length portion of the segment expressed in frames.

lengthSamples.description: The length portion of the segment expressed in samples.

lengthSeconds.description: The length portion of the segment expressed in seconds.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

row.description: Named tuple of all the parameter or column values describing the segment.

speed.description: The speed multiplier of the segment.

index.description: The numeric index of this segment.

Sequence.description: An object describing and controlling a set of sequential parameters . Accessed via

blockSize.description: Get or set the sequence blocksize, which is the number of ParGroups in the block. Changing this will add ParGroups to or remove ParGroups from the sequence.

blocks.description: The set of all blocks in this sequence. A block is a set of parameters which can be repeated in an operator. See SequenceBlock class.

maxBlocks.description: The maximum number of blocks allowed in the sequence, or None if limitless.

name.description: Get or set the sequence name, which affects all its parameter names.

blockName.description: Get the base name of the block name parameter. By default this is 'blockname' for builtin sequences and 'Blockname' for custom sequences.

numBlocks.description: Get or set the total number of parameter blocks in this sequence.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

blockPars.description: An intermediate parameter collection object, from which specific sequence parameters can be found. Returns a list of one parameter from each block. n . seq . Info . blockPars . Tx n . seq . Info . blockPars . Tx [ 3 ] #Par from 4th block. n . seq . Info . blockPars [ 'Tx' ] #returns None if not found.

blockParGroups.description: An intermediate parGroup collection object, from which specific sequence parameter groups can be found. Returns a list of one parGroup from each block. n . seq . Info . blockParGroups . T n . seq . Info . blockParGroups . T [ 3 ] # ParGroup from 4th block. n . seq . Info . blockParGroups [ 'T' ] #returns None if not found.

sequencePar.description: The main sequence parameter defining this sequence.

[].description: Sequence blocks may be easily accessed using the [] subscript and assignment operators. block index - The index of the desired block.

destroyBlock.description: Destroy the block of parameters at the given location. block - The index of the existing block to destroy.

insertBlock.description: Insert a block of parameters at the given location. block - The index of the new block to insert. Returns the newly created block.

moveBlock.description: Move one or more blocks of parameters to the given location. blockFrom - The index or name of the new block to move. SequenceBlock objects also accepted. blockTo - The index or name of the new block to move. SequenceBlock objects also accepted. num - (Keyword, Optional) The number of blocks to move.

reorderBlocks.description: Reorder the specified blocks, leaving the rest in place. indexes - Integer indexes of the blocks to reorder, in their new order. n . seq . Info . reorderBlocks ( 1 , 0 ) # swaps position of first two blocks

sortBlocks.description: Sorts the blocks based on the given key function or baseName parameter. If both are provided, an exception will be raised. key - A function that is passed every block in the sequence and which returns a sortable value. By default it evaluates the block's name parameter, if defined, otherwise it sorts by the first parameter of the block. baseName - If specified, sort by the values of the parameter with this name. reverse - If True, reverses the order of the sort. n . seq . Info . sortBlocks ( rev

SequenceBlock.description: The SequenceBlock class can be used to access the parGroups of a specific block (set of parGroups) in a sequence.

index.description: The numeric index of the block.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

par.description: An intermediate parameter collection object, from which a specific sequence parameter can be found. Names do not include sequence info prefix. n . seq . Info . blocks [ 2 ] . par . Tx # raises exception if not found n . seq . Info . blocks [ 2 ] . parGroup [ 'Tx' ] #returns None if not found

parGroup.description: An intermediate parameter collection object, from which a specific sequence parameter group can be found. Names do not include sequence info prefix. n . seq . Info . blocks [ 2 ] . par . T # raises exception if not found n . seq . Info . blocks [ 2 ] . parGroup [ 'T' ] #returns None if not found

name.description: Get or set the sequence block name parameter value. This is implemented as a specifically named parameter in each block. The name is also used to identify a block by string via Sequence[<block name>].

namePar.description: The parameter defining the name for this block, or None if there isn't one.

sequence.description: The sequence object this block belongs to.

SequenceCollection.description: The SequenceCollection class can be used to access sequences by name.

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

SysInfo.description: The SysInfo class describes current system information. Note: It can be accessed with the sysinfo object, found in the automatically imported td module .

numCPUs.description: The number of CPUs/cores on the system.

xres.description: The system's current monitor resolution width.

yres.description: The system's current monitor resolution height.

tfs.description: The path to the TFS directory.

MIDIInputs.description: A list of all MIDI Input device names.

MIDIOutputs.description: A list of all MIDI Output device names.

GPUName.description: The name of the selected GPU .

GPUID.description: The UUID of the selected GPU .

GPUPlatformID.description: The OS identifier of the selected GPU .

TDU.description: The TDU class is a generic utility class containing all miscellaneous utility functions and members that don't refer specifically to TouchDesigner data structures. To access members and methods of this class use the default instance tdu .

fileTypes.description: A dictionary of all supported file types, organized by category. # example of various file types accepted by Movie File In TOP tdu . fileTypes [ 'movie' ] tdu . fileTypes [ 'image' ] # other file types tdu . fileTypes [ 'audio' ] Note: Acceptable file types can be both uppercase and lowercase, so if suffix is a suffix string, you need to force it to lowercase by using suffix.lower() : for suffix . lower () in tdu . fileTypes [ 'movie' ]: print ( suffix )

FileInfo.description: The FileInfo object takes a file path and has a few utility properties to provide additional information. It is derived from str, so will work as a Python string, but can be differentiated from a regular string by using isinstance(tdu.FileInfo) . Utility properties include: path: filepath string ext: string after and including "." fileType: the TD filetype (from tdu.fileTypes) absPath: the absolute path to filepath dir: the containing directory of filepath exists: exists in file-system isDir: is

debug.description: Helper module for the builtin debug statement. Documentation.

rand.description: Return a random value in the range [0.0, 1.0) given the input seed value. That is, it will never return 1.0, but it may return 0.0. For a given seed, it will always return the same random number. The seed does not need to be a number. If the seed is not numeric, it resolves it to its string representation to produce a unique value. In the case of OPs for example, its string representation is a constant path. Thus one can produce a unique random value for each OP which remains the same for that O

clamp.description: Returns the input value clamped between min and max values. Arguments can be any type that can be compared (float, int, str, etc).

remap.description: Returns the input value remapped from the first range to the second. tdu . remap ( 0.5 , 0 , 1 , - 180 , 180 ) #remap slider value to angle range

base.description: Returns the beginning portion of the string occurring before any digits. The search begins after the last slash if any are present. str - The string to extract the base name from. tdu . base ( 'arm123' ) # returns 'arm' tdu . base ( 'arm123/leg456' ) # returns 'leg' Note this method will work on any string, but when given a specific operator, its more efficient to use its local base member: n = op ( 'arm123/leg456' ) b = n . base #returns 'leg'

digits.description: Returns the numeric value of the last consecutive group of digits in the string, or None if not found. The search begins after the last slash if any are present. The digits do not nessearily need to be at the end of the string. tdu . digits ( 'arm123' ) # returns 123 tdu . digits ( 'arm123/leg456' ) # returns 456 tdu . digits ( 'arm123/leg' ) # returns None, searching is only done after the last / tdu . digits ( 'arm123/456leg' ) # returns 456 Note this method will work on any string, but when g

validName.description: Returns a version of the string suitable for an operator name. Converts illegal characters to underscores. Slashes are converted to underscores. To preserve forward slashes, use validPath() instead. tdu . validName ( 'a#bc def' ) # returns 'a_bc_def'

validPath.description: Returns a version of the string suitable for an operator path, including slashes. Converts illegal characters to underscores. tdu . validPath ( '/a#bc d/ef' ) # returns '/a_bc_d/ef'

expand.description: Return a list of the expanded items, following the rules of Pattern Expansion . tdu . expand ( 'A[1-3] B[xyz]' ) # return ['A1', 'A2', 'A3', 'Bx', 'By', 'Bz']

expandPath.description: Expand the file path, using project.paths, the current folder, and any other relevant information. tdu . expandPath ( 'movies:/test.bmp' ) # looks at project.paths for 'movies' entry.

collapsePath.description: Collapse the file path, using project.paths, the current folder, and any other relevant information. tdu . collapsePath ( 'C:/downloads/test.bmp' ) # looks at project.paths for any entries matching the path, and removes current folder from prefix. path - The path to be shortened. asExpression - (Keyword, Optional) If True, result can be used as an expression, including App Class members and quoted strings.

split.description: Return a list from a space separated string, allowing quote delimiters. string - Any Python object, as it will be evaluated as str(string). Parameters will work. eval - (Keyword, Optional) If True convert any valid Python literal structures: strings, numbers, tuples, lists, dicts, booleans, and None. split ( '1 2.3 None fred "one \' 2 \' " "[1,2]"' ) #yields ['1', '2.3', 'None', 'fred', "one '2'", '[1, 2]'] split ( '1 2.3 None fred "one \' 2 \' " "[1,2]"' , True ) #yields [1, 2.3, None, 'fred',

match.description: Return a subset of inputList, in which each element matches the pattern. Wildcards are supported. tdu . match ( 'foo*' , [ 'foo' , 'bar' ]) # return ['foo'] tdu . match ( 'ba?' , [ 'foo' , 'bar' ]) # return ['bar']

forceCrash.description: forces a crash for debugging and crash recovery purposes

tryExcept.description: Evaluate the first function (func1). If an exception is raised, return second argument instead. Second argument can be either a function, which will be called, or a final result. Note: If the second argument is a function, it is only called if the first function fails. func1: the function that will be "tried" func2val: a value (returned if func1 fails) or a function (return result if func1 fails) This is a one-liner try/except function for use in parameter expressions to handle simple errors. Ti

ParMenu.description: This method uses a list of strings to create an object meant to be used as a parameter menu source. menuNames - A list of strings for menu values. menuLabels - (Optional) A list of strings for menu labels. Defaults to menuNames.

TableMenu.description: Create a parameter menu source object based on a DAT table. This method uses a table to create an object meant to be used as a parameter menu source. table - a DAT table to get the menu information from nameCol - (Keyword, Optional) Column name or number for menuNames. Defaults to 0. labelCol - (Keyword, Optional) Column name or number for menuLabels. Defaults to None, which means to use names as labels. includeFirstRow - (Keyword, Optional) if True, include first row of table in menu entries. D

isPointCloudFile.description: Check if the input file is a point cloud file. For .exr, the channel headers are checked. Returns true if channels ending in X,Y,Z or x,y,z are found, or if the file was saved out using Movie File Out TOP with "Save as Point Cloud" set. For all other types, only the file extension is checked. path - Path to the input file.

parSummary.description: Returns a dictionary describing the builtin parameters of the OP class provided. OPType - The OP type to describe. tdu . parSummary ( mathCHOP ) # Works on td.OP types or strings, not actual operator instances.

TOP.description: A TOP describes a reference to a TOP operator.

aspect.description: Texture aspect ratio, width divided by height.

depth.description: Texture depth, when using a 3 dimensional texture.

gpuMemory.description: The amount of GPU memory this TOP is using, in bytes.

curPass.description: The current cooking pass iteration, beginning at 0. The total can be set with the 'Passes' parameter on the operator's common page.

isTOP.description: True if the operators is a TOP .

newestSliceWOffset.description: When a Texture3D TOP fills it's contents, it keeps track of the newest slice it's filled so texturing can be offset this so a '0' coordinate results in the first slice. This member give you access to this value.

pixelFormat.description: Returns the pixel Format of the TOP . The returned string format resembles the pixel format on the operator's common page. This value is only useful for display purposes, not for python handling or interacting with parms. For the latter, use pixelFormatName .

pixelFormatName.description: Returns the menuName corresponding to the pixel format of the TOP . This value should be used for all python handling, interacting with parameters etc, not the value returned by pixelFormat . The latter should only be used for display purposes.

sample.description: Returns a 4-tuple representing the color value at the specified texture location. One horizontal and one vertical component must be specified. Note that this is a very expensive operation currently. It will always stall the graphics pipeline if the TOP is currently queued to get updated, and then downloads the entire texture (not just the requested pixel). Use this for debugging and non-realtime workflows only. x - (Keyword, Optional) The horizontal pixel coordinate to be sampled. y - (Keyword,

numpyArray.description: Returns the TOP image as a Python NumPy array. Note that since NumPy arrays are referenced by line first, pixels are addressed as [h, w]. Currently data will always be in floating point, regardless of what the texture data format is on the GPU . delayed - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, the download results will be delayed until the next call to numpyArray(), avoiding stalling the GPU waiting for the result immediately. This is useful to avoid long stalls that occur if immediately asking for

save.description: Saves the image to the file system. Support file formats are: .tif , .tiff , .jpg , .jpeg , .bmp , .png , .exr and .dds . Returns a FileSaveStatus Class object that can be cast to a str to return filename and path used, and the isCompleted() member can be queried to check if file finished saving which is useful for asynchronous saving. filepath - (Optional) The path and filename to save to. If not given then a default filename will be used, and the file will be saved in the project.folder folder

saveByteArray.description: Saves the image to a bytearray object in the requested file format. Support file formats are: .tif, .tiff, .jpg, .jpeg, .bmp, .png, .exr and .dds. Returns the bytearray object. To get the raw image data use numpyArray() or cudaArray() instead. filetype - (Optional) A string specifying the file type to save as. If not given the default file type '.tiff' will be used. Just the suffix of the string is used to determine the file type. E.g '.tiff', 'file.tiff', 'C:/Files/file.tiff' will all work. Suf

cudaMemory.description: Copies the contents of the TOP to a newly allocated block of raw CUDA memory. The CUDA memory will be deallocated when the returned CUDAMemory object is deallocated. Ensure you keep a reference to the returned object around as long as you are using it. stream - (Optional) A CUDA stream handle to synchronize the operation with. Any CUDA subsequent operations occurring on this stream will wait for this CUDA memory to be filled before executing their operation. If this is left as None, then the def

TextLine.description: A line of text in the Text TOP or Text SOP , after it has been formatted. It is also used by the Geo Text COMP to return the position and glyphs for a block of text. Contains various members about the line such as it's text, position etc."

glyph.description: The index of the glyph that represents this text line.

fontIndex.description: The index of the font that the glyph belongs to. Glyphs are not interchangable between fonts.

origin.description: A tdu.Position object that gives the baseline origin of the line of text.

lineWidth.description: The width of the format box of this line of text. This member is not used by the Geo Text COMP .

TextTOP.description: This class inherits from the TOP class . It references a specific Text TOP .

curText.description: Current text contents, when used with a Field COMP .

cursorEnd.description: Get or set cursor end position, when used with a Field COMP .

cursorStart.description: Get or set cursor start position, when used with a Field COMP .

selectedText.description: Selected contents, when used with a Field COMP .

textHeight.description: Calculated height of text, in pixels. This value does not changes based on the particular of characters in the string. It only depends on the number of lines, line spacing, positioning and font metrics of the font.

textWidth.description: Calculated width of text, in pixels. This value does change depending on the particular characters in the string. Different characters have difference advance widths, and this value is the sum of all the advance widths of the characters.

numLines.description: Get the number of lines of the outputted text, after operations such as word-wrap have been applied.

ascender.description: The ascender of the font, as described by the font's metrics.

descender.description: The descender of the font, as described by the font's metrics.

capHeight.description: The cap height of the font, as described by the font's metrics. This is usually the height of a capital H.

xHeight.description: The x height of the font, as described by the font's metrics. This is usually the height of a lower case x.

lineGap.description: The suggested gap between lines, as described by the font's metrics.

fontSupportsChars.description: Returns True if every character maps to a glyph. This doesn't mean the font supports the language in all cases. Glyphs that come from ligatures etc. may still be missing from the font. str - The string to be analyzed.

evalTextSize.description: Returns a tuple of width, height of the given string using the operators settings. Does not include the word wrap and auto size font options. str - The string to be measured.

lines.description: Returns a list of TextLine Class objects. This list of lines is formed after operations such as word-wrap have been applied.

Timecode.description: The Timecode class holds a timecode value. See also Timecode and Timecode CHOP .

countdown.description: Return a Timecode Object of the difference between the length and the current time. If a custom length is not specified then it will use a default: 23:59:59:ff for SMPTE and 99:59:59:ff.

dropFrame.description: True if the Timecode is drop-frame, False otherwise.

fps.description: Get or set the framerate (in frames per second) of the Timecode.

hour.description: The Timecode hour: 0 to 99 if non-SMPTE, 0 to 23 otherwise.

negative.description: True if the Timecode is negative, and False otherwise. Always False if the Timecode is following SMPTE standard.

smpte.description: True if the Timecode is SMPTE standard, and False otherwise. SMPTE Timecodes cannot be negative and cannot exceed 24 hours.

text.description: Get the text format of the Timecode.

totalFrames.description: The total number of Timecode frames, which is calculated from the hour, minute, second, frame values. Whether or not the Timecode is drop frame will also affect the value.

totalSeconds.description: The total number of Timecode seconds, which is calculated from the hour, minute, second, frame values. Whether or not the Timecode is drop frame will also affect the value.

cycle.description: Get or set whether the timecode cycles. True if the Timecode cycles when the timecode value reaches the custom length (ie. specified with setLength()). If False then the timecode value will hold the last value (ie. length).

setComponents.signature: setComponents(hour, minute, second, frame, negative=False, fps=None)
setComponents.description: Set the Timecode from individual time components. hour - The new hour value. minute - The new minute value. second - The new second value. frame - The new frame value. negative (Keyword, Optional) - Whether the new Timecode is negative. False by default. fps (Keyword, Optional) - The Timecode's FPS . If not specified then the FPS will not change. autoDropFrame - (Keyword, Optional) When enabled, will automatically calculate drop-frames for fractional rates (eg. 29.97). True by default. n . setCo

setString.description: Set the Timecode from a string formated as [-]hh:mm:ss:ff. timecodeStr - The string in the format: [-]hh:mm:ss:ff. fps (Keyword, Optional) - The Timecode's FPS . If not specified then the FPS will not change. n . setString ( '01:01:00:00' , fps = 60 )

setTotalFrames.description: Set the Timecode to a single integer value representing the new total frames. totalFrames - The new total frame value. fps (Keyword, Optional) - The Timecode's FPS . If not specified then the FPS will not change. n . setTotalFrames ( 120 , fps = 60 ) # new Timecode will be 00:00:02:00

setLength.description: Set Timecode to a custom length. Useful in conjunction with countdown. length - The new length, either a total frame value or a Timecode Object . Must be above 0. n . setLength ( 600 ) # sets the length to 10 seconds for a Timecode with 60 FPS .

UI.description: The UI class describes access to the UI elements of the application, found in the automatically imported td module .

clipboard.description: Get or set the operating system clipboard text contents.

dpiBiCubicFilter.description: Get or set the global DPI scale filtering mode of TouchDesigner windows. True means bi-cubic, False means linear.

masterVolume.description: Get or set the master audio output volume. A value of 0 is no output, while a value of 1 is full output.

panes.description: Access to the set of all panes .

performMode.description: Get or set Perform Mode . Set to True to go into Perform Mode , False to go into Designer Mode .

preferences.description: Access to the application preferences , which can also be access through the Preferences Dialog .

redrawMainWindow.description: Get or set whether the main window should redraw. The main window is either the main network editor, or the perform window.

rolloverOp.description: Operator currently under the mouse in a network editor.

rolloverPar.description: Parameter currently under the mouse in a parameter dialog.

rolloverParGroup.description: ParGroup currently under the mouse in a parameter dialog.

rolloverPanel.description: returns the panel currently under the mouse.

rolloverPage.description: returns the Page currently under the mouse in a parameter dialog.

rollover.description: combines the other rollover members into one test, reporting the TD object under the mouse. Because there is some overlap, priority is in the following order: Par | ParGroup | Page | OP | Panel . Note: Panel and OP tests both return a PanelCOMP, so to tell the difference between rolling over a PanelCOMP operator in a network and rolling over an active Panel , you may have to use the specific rolloverPanel or rolloverOP methods.

lastChopChannelSelected.description: Last CHOP channel selected via mouse.

showPaletteBrowser.description: Get or set display of the palette browser.

status.description: Get or set the status message. ui . status = 'Operation Complete'

undo.description: The Undo object, which provides access to application undo functions.

windowX.description: Get the app window X position.

windowY.description: Get the app window Y position.

copyOPs.description: Copy a list of operators to the operator clipboard. All operators must be children of the same component. listOfOPs - A list containing one or more OPs to be copied. ui . copyOPs ( op ( 'geo1' ) . selected )

pasteOPs.description: Copy the contents of the operator clipboard into the specified component. COMP - The destination to receive the operators. x - Optional network coordinates at which to paste the operators. y - see x l = ui . pasteOPs ( op ( 'geo2' ) )

messageBox.description: This method will open a message dialog box with the specified message. Returns the index of the button clicked. title - Specifies the window title. message - Specifies the content of the dialog. buttons - (Keyword, Optional) Specifies a list button labels to show in the dialog. # basic usage ui . messageBox ( 'Warning' , 'Have a nice day.' ) # specify options and report result a = ui . messageBox ( 'Please select:' , 'Buttons:' , buttons = [ 'a' , 'b' , 'c' ]) ui . messageBox ( 'Results' , 'You

refresh.description: Update and redraw all viewports, nodes, UI elements etc immediately. This update is otherwise done once per frame at the end of all script executions. For example, if the current frame is manually changed during a script, a call to refresh will cause all dependent data to update immediately. for i in range ( 100 ): ui . status = str ( i ) ui . refresh ()

chooseFile.description: Open a dialog box for loading or saving a file. Returns the filename selected or None if the dialog is cancelled. load - (Keyword, Optional) If set to True, the dialog will be a Load dialog, otherwise it's a Save dialog. start - (Keyword, Optional) If provided, specifies an initial folder location and/or filename selection. fileTypes - (Keyword, Optional) If provided, specifies a list of file extensions that can be used as filters. Otherwise '*.*' is the only filter. asExpression - (Keyword, Opt

chooseFolder.description: Open a dialog box for selecting a folder. Returns the folder selected or None if the dialog is cancelled. title - (Keyword, Optional) If provided, specifies the window title. start - (Keyword, Optional) If provided, specifies an initial folder location and/or filename selection. asExpression - (Keyword, Optional) If set to true, the results are provided as an expression, suitable for a Parameter expression or as input to an eval() call. App Class member constants such as samplesFolder may be inc

viewFile.description: View a URL or file in the default external application. You can use ui.viewFile() to open a folder/directory in Windows Explorer or macOS Finder. URL_or_path - URL or path to launch. a = ui . viewFile ( 'output.txt' ) showInFolder - Show file as selected in Explorer or macOS Finder instead of launching an external application. a = ui . viewFile ( 'output.txt' , showInFolder = True )

openCOMPEditor.description: Open component editor for the specific operator. path - Specifies the path to the operator. An OP can be passed in as well.

openDialogHelp.description: Open help page for the specific dialog. title - Specifies the help page to open. ui . openDialogHelp ( ' Window Placement Dialog ' )

openExportMovie.description: Open the Export Movie Dialog . path - Specifies the operator content to export, optional. ui . openExportMovie ( '/project1/out1' )

openMIDIDeviceMapper.description: Open the MIDI Device Mapper Dialog .

openOperatorSnippets.description: Open the Operator Snippets window. optype - (Keyword, Optional) Specify the operator by python operator class type to open. example - (Keyword, Optional) Specify the numeric example for the available operator snippets.

openVersion.description: Open a dialog displaying current version information. See also: App.version

findEditDAT.description: Given an external filename, finds the corresponding DAT thats update from this filename if any..

Undo.description: A class to enable and disable undo functionality. Undo blocks can be created in scripts. At the end of callbacks, any dangling undo blocks will be terminated. See examples at bottom of page.

globalState.description: Is global undo enabled or not.

redoStack.description: A list of names for redo operations available.

undoStack.description: A list of names for undo operations available.

startBlock.description: Start a named undo block. While the block is active, any script changes to TouchDesigner objects that are undoable in the editor will be added to the undo stack. name - The name of the block, which should briefly describe the change that would be undone. enable - In the rare case you want to insert a block that doesn't store undo info within a block that does, you can create a block with enable off. Example: # undoing this will remove the noiseTOP and noiseSOP only. ui . undo . startBlock ( "con

clear.description: Clear undo and redo stack. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

addCallback.description: Add a Python callback into the current undo block. The callback should be defined as def yourCallbackName ( isUndo , info ) . The isUndo argument tells if the call is an undo or a redo (the same callback is used for both) and the info argument is the same Python object passed to addCallback , and should contain any information needed to perform the undo/redo. callback - user defined callback function. Should be defined as callback ( isUndo , info ) info - this argument will be passed back to use

redo.description: Redo the next operation. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

undo.description: Undo the last operation. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

VFS.description: The VFS Class describes a COMP 's Virtual File System . To access a virtual file in any operator's file parameter, use the virtual path format: vfs:<path to comp>:<filename> . VFSFile_Class does the file operators.

owner.description: Get the OP owner.

[].description: VFS Files may be easily accessed using the [] syntax. name - Must be an exact VFS file name. Wildcards are not supported. If not found, an error will be raised. p = op ( 'base1' ) . vfs [ 'Banana.tif' ]

addByteArray.description: Add an embedded file from a bytearray to the component. Returns a VFSFile instance of the added file. To delete the file, see destroy() on VFSFile Class . byteArray - A bytearray or bytes object representing the contents of the file. name - The name of the file on VFS .

addFile.description: Add an embedded file from disk to the component with an option to override the name. Returns a VFSFile instance of the added file. To delete the file, see destroy() on VFSFile Class . filePath - The path of the file on disk to add. overrideName (Keyword, Optional) - When specified, will override the name of the file in VFS .

export.description: Exports any matching files to the folder on disk. If overwrite is True then any existing files on disks with the same name will be overwritten. Returns a list of paths on disk to the exported files. folder - The folder on disk to export the files to. pattern (Keyword, Optional) - The pattern to match names by. overwrite (Keyword, Optional) - When True, will overwrite any files that share the same name. # VFS contains one file with name 'A/B.tif' COMP . vfs . export ( 'C:/tmp' ) # returns ['C:/tm

find.description: Finds all files in VFS with names matching the pattern. Returns a list of VFSFile objects. pattern (Keyword, Optional) - The pattern to match names by.

len.description: Returns the total number of virtual files. a = len ( op ( 'base1' ) . vfs )

Iterator.description: Iterate over each virtual file name. for f in op ( 'base1' ) . vfs : debug ( f ) # print info of all virtual files on base1

VFSFile.description: The VFSFile Class describes a virtual file contained within a Virtual File System . To access a virtual file in any operator's file parameter, use the virtual path as described below in the virtualPath member.

name.description: Get or set the name of the file. This name can include slashes but should not include leading slashes.

size.description: Get the size of the file data.

date.description: Get the modified date of the file in the form of a datetime Python object.

virtualPath.description: Get the virtual path of the file. Returns a String formatted for fetching the file data from VFS in operators such as the Movie File In TOP . Format is vfs:<path to owner>:<filename> .

originalFilePath.description: Get the original file path on disk. If the VFSFile was created from a bytearray and not a file on disk then this will be empty.

owner.description: Get the OP owner.

byteArray.description: Get or set the file data as a bytearray.

destroy.description: Destroys the file in VFS referenced by this object.

export.description: Exports the file to the specified folder on disk and returns the location. folder - The folder on disk to export the file to.

Vector.description: The vector class holds a single 3 component vector. A vector describes a direction in space, and it's important to use a vector or Position as appropriate for the data that is being calculated. When being multiplied by a Matrix , this class will implicitly have a 4th component (W component) of 0. A ...

x.description: Gets or sets the X component of the vector.

y.description: Gets or sets the Y component of the vector.

z.description: Gets or sets the Z component of the vector.

angle.description: Returns the angel (in degrees) between the current vector and specified vector (vec). d = v . angle ( v2 )

scale.description: Scales each component of the vector by the specified values. x, y, z - The values to scale each component of the vector by. v . scale ( 1 , 2 , 1 )

normalize.description: Makes the length of this vector 1. m . normalize ()

length.description: Returns the length of this vector. l = m . length ()

lengthSquared.description: Returns the squared length of this vector. l = v . lengthSquared ()

copy.description: Returns a new vector that is a copy of the vector. newV = v . copy ()

distance.description: Returns the distance of the current vector to specified vector (vec). l = v . distance ( v2 )

lerp.description: Returns the linear interpolation of this vector and vec2. That is vec1 * (1.0 - t) + vec2 * t, where vec1 is the current vector. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1]. l = v . lerp ( v2 , t )

slerp.description: Returns the spherical interpolation of this vector and vec2. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1]. l = v . slerp ( v2 , t )

dot.description: Returns the dot product of this vector and the passed vector. vec - The other vector to use to calculate the dot product d = v . dot ( otherV )

cross.description: Returns the cross product of this vector and the passed vector. The operation is self cross vec. vec - The other vector to use to calculate the cross product. c = v . cross ( otherV )

project.description: Projects this vector onto the plan defined by vec1 and vec2. Both vec1 and vec2 must be normalized. The result may not be normalized. vec1, vec2 - The vectors that specify the plane to project onto. Must be normalized. v . project ( v1 , v2 )

reflect.description: Reflects the current vector about the specified vector (vec). v . reflect ( v2 )

Vertex.description: A Vertex describes an instance to a single geometry vertex, contained within a Prim object.

index.description: The vertex position in its primitive .

owner.description: The OP to which this object belongs.

point.description: Get or set the point to which the vertex refers.

prim.description: The prim to which the vertex belongs.
